OpenVPN Introduction
According to the IP address assigned to the client of the gateway by OpenVPN server, you can enter the IP address of the gateway client directly into the "Device IP" item in the login interface of the configuration software, and click Connect to log in the gateway.
Alarm and Event Configuration
Click "Alarms", move the mouse to the right box, right click, click "Add", to enter "Alarm and Event" setting box. You can configure the data points, action and the action to be performed for alarm recovery.
Alarm Point Configuration
Alarm Event Configuration
Put mouse in "Alarm triggered execution action", right click the prompt box, click "Add" to enter event configuration box, and set the operation to be performed when the alarm is triggered. In the same way, put mouse on "Alarm recovery execution action", set operations when the alarm release.
Tasks Configuration
Left click on "Task", move the mouse to the right box, click the right mouse, "Add" will pop up, click "Add", to enter task schedule setting box, put the mouse in the box, and right click to enter the operation box, click "Add", to enter Execution Event box.
Data Service
Transparent Transmission
To configure the transparent transmission protocol, first set the COM mode to “Transparent” and configure the COM parameters. Then configure the parameters of the transparent transmission protocol for it to take effect.
All 8 COM ports can be used for transparent transmission, and the steps are the same. However, only one COM port can be set for transparent transmission at a time, simultaneous transparent transmission on all 8 COM ports is not supported.
Example (using COM2 for transparent transmission):
Select Transparent Mode for COM2.
Configure the COM2 attribute parameters and click OK.
Go to “Data Service” → “Transparent” to complete the settings.
Modbus RTU to Modbus TCP
To configure Modbus RTU to Modbus TCP, first set the COM mode to “Modbus RTU to Modbus TCP” and configure the COM parameters. Then configure the parameters for Modbus RTU to Modbus TCP to take effect.
All 8 COM ports can be set to use Modbus RTU to Modbus TCP, but only one COM port can be active for this function at a time, it cannot run on all 8 COM ports simultaneously.
Example (using COM2 for Modbus RTU to Modbus TCP):
Select Modbus RTU to Modbus TCP mode for COM2.
Configure the COM2 parameters and click OK.
Go to “Data Service” → “Modbus RTU to Modbus TCP” to complete the settings.
IEC104
The gateway supports IEC104 protocol and functions as an IEC104 server to provide data externally. It converts data from other protocols into IEC104-format remote telesignals, telemetry, and telecontrol data. This enables upper-level systems that support the IEC104 protocol to communicate with devices using other protocols via the gateway.
The supported IEC104 type identifiers include:
01 – Single-point information (M_SP_NA_1)
09 – Measured value, normalized (M_ME_NA_1)
11 – Measured value, scaled (M_ME_NB_1)
13 – Measured value, short floating point (M_ME_NC_1)
45 – Single command (C_SC_NA_1)
46 – Double command (C_DC_NA_1)
48 – Setpoint command, normalized (C_SE_NA_1)
49 – Setpoint command, scaled (C_SE_NB_1)
Note:
1, Telemetry upload supports the following types: normalized (M_ME_NA_1), scaled (M_ME_NB_1), and short floating point (M_ME_NC_1). The telemetry upload mode is determined by the data point configuration, and only one telemetry mode can be selected per data point.
2, Telecontrol supports both single-point and double-point commands. Control commands are executed immediately upon issuance.
Local Network Mode
Only the port number needs to be configured (default: 2404); there is no need to enable "IEC to Cloud". The IP address used is the WAN port IP, and IEC104 data is transmitted upstream via the WAN port. The master station must be on the same local network as the WAN port.
Cloud Platform Mode
To upload IEC104 data to the cloud, the "IEC to Cloud" feature must be enabled.
The port field does not need to be filled in; the default port number 2404 is retained.
IEC104 data can be uploaded to the cloud via the WAN port or 4G connection.
The configuration for "IEC to Cloud" is explained below. For other configuration operations, please refer to the LAN mode instructions.
3.2.12 BLRMS Services
To use the BLRMS service, you must first configure the "BLRMS Management Platform" in Advanced Settings.
BE116 Gateway Application Example
Collecting Metering Devices
Metering Collection via COM Port
The COM port currently only supports collecting data from electricity meters using the DL/T645-2007 protocol.
COM Port Configuration
Using COM to Collect Data from Electricity Meters using the DL/T645-2007 Protocol as an Example
(1) Double-click on "COM1".
(2) Select mode: Collection.
(3) Since it's for collecting data from electricity meters, select "DLT645" from the dropdown menu for device brand, and "DL/T645-2007" for device model. Adjust the polling interval and communication timeout according to requirements. It's recommended to set the polling interval and timeout to over 2 seconds if the baud rate is low and there are many data points.
(4) Configure baud rate, stop bits, data bits, and parity according to the parameters set on the COM port of the electricity meter, ensuring consistency.
(5) Click "OK".
Note: Clicking "Write Configuration" will automatically restart the gateway device. After the restart, the configuration for COM1 port will take effect.
Adding Electricity Meters via COM Port
Click on "COM1", then right-click, and select "Add".
Enter any name for the device, for example, "DL/T645".
Enter the communication address, which corresponds to the address field or meter number of the electricity meter.
Click "OK" to add the electricity meter device.
Note: After clicking "OK", the added device will be displayed under COM1. If you want to add multiple devices, repeat steps (1) to (4).
Note: Clicking "Write Configuration" will automatically restart the gateway device. After the restart, the electricity meter devices added under COM1 will be successfully added.
Add Electricity Meters Datapoints
(1) Click on "DL/T645", move the mouse cursor to the box, right-click, and then click "Add".
(2) Variable Name: Name the data point of the meter, for example: "Phase A Voltage".
(3) The identifier of the data point can be filled arbitrarily, but it must be unique. For example, if collecting data for "Phase A Voltage", the MQTT identifier can be filled as "REG001".
(4) Choose the collection address format according to the requirements and fill in with OCT/DEC/HEX for octal/decimal/hexadecimal respectively. Do not fill in the meter collection address, select decimal.
(5) Address Type: Select the data point of the meter to be collected from the dropdown menu. For example, if you want to collect data for "Phase A Voltage", then choose "Phase A Voltage".
(6) Address: Not applicable in the meter protocol, should not be filled in.
(7) Data Type: Data collected from the meter in the gateway is stored as 32-bit single-precision floating-point.
(8) Add Number: Not applicable in the meter protocol, should not be filled in.
(9) Read/Write Type: Read-only.
(10) Ratio: Specify how much to multiplying or reducing the data before uploading it to the platform, fill in according to requirements.
(11) Map Address: Specify the address where the data collected from the meter will be stored in the gateway. This can be filled arbitrarily, but mapping addresses must be unique. Range: 0-2000. For example, if collecting data for "Phase A Voltage", the mapping address can be "10". In the configuration software, mapping addresses are represented as Modbus addresses outside the brackets, while inside the brackets, M.XXX represents the PLC Modbus address.
(12) Variable Unit: Fill in as needed, optional.
(13) IEC Address Type (Uplink): Select the type of data to be converted to IEC104 data, such as converting data for Phase A Voltage to IEC104 telemetry data.
(14) IOA: The address where the converted IEC104 data is stored. For example, the IOA for Phase A Voltage is "16385".
(15) Click "OK".
Note: After clicking "OK", the data point will be displayed in the box as shown above. If you want to continue adding data points, right-click inside the box, click "Add" to open the data point configuration box, and repeat steps (2) to (15).
Note: Clicking "Write Configuration" will automatically restart the gateway. After restarting, the collected data points from the meter will take effect.
Add IEC104 Device to Ethernet Port
Both WAN and LAN ports can collect data from IEC104 devices, either by direct connection or through a switch. When collecting data from IEC104 devices, the device's data points cannot exceed 1000 telesignals inputs and 1000 telemetry inputs.
Multiple public addresses cannot be collected simultaneously. Supported functional types for data collection include: 01 Single-point telesignals input (M_SP_NA_1), 03 Double-point telesignals input (M_DP_NA_1), 13 Short floating-point telemetry input (M_ME_NC_1), 45 Single-point telecontrol (C_SC_NA_1), and 46 Double-point telecontrol (C_DC_NA_1).
WAN Port Configuration
This example demonstrates collecting IEC104 data via the WAN port through a switch, with the switch connected to the external network. The configuration for the WAN port is as follows:
(1) Double-click on "WAN".
(2) Auto IP: Specify whether the WAN port should automatically obtain an IP address. By default, this is enabled. Set according to your requirements. In this example, the switch is connected to a router that automatically assigns IP addresses, so keep this option enabled.
(3) IP Address: The gateway obtains its IP address from the router. If a specific IP address is required, set it accordingly. The IEC104 devices and the gateway should be on the same LAN.
(4) Subnet Mask: Specify the subnet mask for the WAN port of the gateway. If a specific IP address is required, set it accordingly.
(5) Gateway: The gateway obtains the gateway address from the router. If a specific IP address is required, set it accordingly.
(6) MAC Address: The MAC address of the gateway.
(7) DNS: The DNS obtained by the gateway from the router. If a specific IP address is required, set it accordingly.
(8) Click "OK".
Note: Clicking "Write Configuration" will automatically restart the gateway. After restarting, the configuration changes for the WAN port will take effect.
Attention: The IP address of the WAN port specifies which IP address segment the WAN port device belongs to. If the IP address of the WAN port device does not belong to the IP segment set for the WAN port, data collection via the WAN port will not work. You may need to change either the IP address of the WAN port or the IP address of the WAN port device according to your requirements. Changes to the gateway's IP address will take effect only after writing configuration and restarting the device.
Add IEC104 Device to WAN Port
(1) Click on "WAN", right-click, then click "Add".
(2) Device Name: Name the device, for example: "IEC104".
(3) Enter the IP address of the acquisition device. For example, if the IEC104 device IP is 192.168.1.158, enter this IP address here. Note that the IEC104 device IP must be in the same network segment as the WAN port IP.
(4) Device Port: Fill in according to your requirements, for example: 2404.
(5) Since it is collecting IEC104 data, select "IEC" for Device Brand and "IEC104" for Device Model.
(6) Fill in the parameters for the IEC104 device, including polling intervals, K, W, T0, T1, T2, T3, according to your requirements, or leave them as default.
(7) Click "OK" to add the IEC104 device.
Note: After clicking "OK", the added device will be displayed under the WAN port. If you want to add multiple devices, repeat steps (1) to (7).
Note: Clicking "Write Configuration" will automatically restart the gateway. After restarting, the IEC104 devices added under the WAN port will be successfully added.
Add IEC104 Device Datapoints
(1) Click on "IEC104", right-click, and then click "Add".
(2) Variable Name: Name the data point, for example: if collecting data for "Short Floating Point Telemetry 16385", it can be named as "M_ME_NC_1".
(3) The identifier of the data point can be filled arbitrarily, but it must be unique. For example, if collecting data for "Short Floating Point Telemetry 16385", the identifier can be filled as "REG005".
(4) Choose the collection address format according to the requirements and fill in the gateway with OCT/DEC/HEX for octal/decimal/hexadecimal respectively. For IEC104 data points, select decimal.
(5) Address Type: Select the type of IEC104 data to be collected, such as telesignals, telemetry, or telecontrol according to your requirements. IEC104 encoding instructions are available in the "Help" section.
(6) Address: IEC104 information object address, for example: Collecting data for "16385" short floating point telemetry.
(7) Data Type: Select the data type based on the selected functional type of the IEC104 device.
(8) Add Number: If collecting continuous addresses, multiple collections can be made from the same register.
(9) Read/Write: Select from "Read Only" or "Read/Write" options.
(10) Map Address: Specify the address where the collected data point will be stored in the gateway device. This can be filled arbitrarily, but mapping addresses must be unique. Range: 0-2000. For example, if collecting data for "Short Floating Point Telemetry 16385", the mapping address can be "20". In the configuration software, mapping addresses are represented as Modbus addresses outside the brackets, while inside the brackets, M.XXX represents the PLC Modbus address.
(11) Variable Unit: Fill in as needed, or leave it blank.
(12) IEC Address Type (Uplink): Select the type of data to be converted to IEC104 data, for example, converting data for "Short Floating Point Telemetry 16385" to IEC104 short floating point telemetry data.
(13) IOA: The address where the converted IEC104 data will be stored. For example, IOA address for "Short Floating Point Telemetry 16385" in the gateway is "16387".
(14) Click "OK".
Note: After clicking "OK", the data point will be displayed in the box as shown above. If you want to continue adding data points, right-click inside the box, click "Add" to open the data point configuration box, and repeat steps (2) to (14).
Note: Clicking "Write Configuration" will automatically restart the gateway. After restarting, the collected IEC104 data points will take effect.
Add Modbus Device
Connect IO Module M140T to BE116 COM2 port and connect 4G RTU S475 to BE116 LAN port. M140T DI DO data is collected from COM2 via Modbus RTU protocol. S475 device data is collected from LAN port via Modbus TCP protocol. Connect BE116 WAN port to industrial router R40 LAN port. Router R40 provides network to BE116 Gateway.
Connect M140T & S475 to BE116
Network Switch is connected to BE116 LAN port. S475 is connected to Switch. S475 device data is collected through LAN via Modbus TCP. M140T data is collected through COM2 via Modbus RTU protocol. Collected data will be sent to various clouds via 4G router R40 with its routing function.
Note: Both WAN and LAN can collect device data. The configuration procedure is the same. This example is introduction to LAN port configuration.
COM Port Configuration
The configuration method of all COM ports is the same. Take COM2 connection as an example.
COM2 Configuration
COM2 collect M140T data via Modbus RTU
Double click “COM2” to enter setting box
Mode Selection: Collection
Device Brand: Modbus; Device Model: Modbus RTU The polling interval and timeout are set by default and set according to requirements.
Baud rate, Stop bit, Data Bit and Parity Bit will be set the same as that in M140T RS485 port
Click OK to confirm
Note: Click Write Configuration. Gateway will restart automatically. COM configuration will be valid after device restarting
Add COM Port Device M140T
Click COM2, right click the mouse and click Add to enter configuration box
Set device name, for example, set M140T as device name
Input device Modbus address, for example, if M140T Modbus ID is 1, put 1
Select Type of data to be collected. The example is to collect the DI and DO of the M140T, both of which are Boolean type, not numeric type register, select as default.
Write function code: As default, M140T supports writing multiple registers.
Click OK to confirm adding M140T
Click COM2 to view the added device M140T. If more devices to be added, repeat Step (1)-(6)
Note: Click Write Configuration. Gateway BE116 will restart automatically. After restarting, M140T is added successfully.
Add M140T Datapoints
Click M140T, move mouse cursor to the right box, right click mouse to enter datapoint setting box.
Variable name: Set datapoint name, for example, DO1
Variable key, which can be filled in arbitrarily. The identifier cannot be repeated, for example, the MQTT identifier of the DO1 data point is filled in as DO1.
Select the acquisition address and choose data format according to the requirements, OCT/DEC/HEX are octal/decimal/hexadecimal respectively. The collected Modbus protocol address is input in decimal, so the example selects decimal.
Address type: Select according to the function codes supported by the collected data points. For example, the DO of the collected M140T supports the "01" function code, so select "01 Coil Status", and DI supports the "02" function code, so select "02 Input Status”
Address: The register address of the data point, such as: data point DO1 is "0" register address in the M140T, so fill in "0".
Data type: Select according to the data point, such as: DI and DO of M140T are both coil types, so select "bool".
Add Number: If it is collecting continuous addresses, the same function code can be collected multiple times.
Read/Write: Automatic identifying read-write type according to Address Type
Map address: Fill in the address where the collected data points are stored to the BE116 gateway device, which can be filled in at will. Mapped addresses cannot be duplicated. Range: 0-2000. For example, the data collected from DO1 is stored in the “0” register address of the gateway. The outside of the mapping address on the configuration software represents the Modbus address, and M.XXX represents the PLC Modbus address.
Variable Unit: Input any required unit or leave it blank.
IEC Address Type(Uplink): Select the type of data to convert to IEC 104 format, such as converting DO data to IEC 104 telecontrol data, and DI data to IEC 104 telesignals data.
IOA: Storage Address for IEC 104 Data: For example, IOA address for DO1 is "24577".
Click OK to confirm
Note: After clicking OK to confirm the configuration, datapoints will appear in the box like above picture. If more datapoints to be added, right click the box and click Add to enter datapoint configuration box, repeat Step (2)-(14)
Note: Click Write Configuration. Gateway will restart automatically. After restarting, M140T datapoints are added successfully
Ethernet Port Configuration
Both WAN and LAN can collect device data. The configuration procedure is the same.
LAN Port Configuration
Below is the example of configuring LAN port to connect S475.
Double click LAN to enter setting box
DHCP: Enable auto IP distribution. Default is disabled. For examples, S475 has been set to auto retrieving IP, then LAN port must enable DHCP.
Routing: Enable network rounting function. Default is disabled. For example, S475 data will be collected without network requirement, then disable routing function
IP Address: defaut is 192.168.3.1, the IP addresses assigned to LAN port devices must be within the range. It can be changed according to requirement. For example, S475 is set to auto retrieving IP and the range is not limited,thus it’s not necessary to change it.
Subnet mask: The subnet mask of the LAN port gateway.
MAC Address: Input LAN port MAC address
Click OK to confirm it
Note: Click Write Configuration and Gateway will restart. Turn off the power of Gateway and restart it. After that LAN port configuration is done successfully
Note: LAN Port IP Address specifies the IP address arrange of LAN port device. If device IP address is not within the range, data can’t be collected. Thus it’s necessary to change LAN port IP address according to requirement. IP Address change will not be effective until gateway is powered off and powered on again.
Add LAN Port Device S475
Click LAN and right click mouse to enter device setting box
Device Name: Such as S475
Device IP: For example, S475 is set to auto retrieving IP. Open S475 configuration software and view its IP(192.168.3.125). Thus input S475 IP 192.168.3.125.
Note: If LAN port IP is changed and LAN port device auto retrieves IP, please click Write Configuration, power off gateway and power it on again. Then IP change can be viewed
Device Port: LAN port device port. For example, S475 Modbus TCP port is 502.
Device Brand: Modbus; Device Model: Modbus TCP
(Gateway collects S475 through LAN port through Modbus TCP protocol)
The polling interval and timeout in the button can be defaulted and filled in according to requirements.
Select Data Type. For example, S475 power source and temperature & humidity data is 16-bit AB type, 32-bit data is not collected. Thus select 16-bit AB type and keep 32-bit data type with default setting
Write function code: choose 15/16, and choose according to the function code supported by the device.
Click OK to confirm the setting
Note: S475 device icon will appear after confirming the configuration. If more devices to be added, repeat Step (1)-(9)
Note: Click Write Configuration and gateway will restart automatically. After restarting, device S475 is added successfully
Add S475 Datapoints
Click S475, move mouse cursor to the right box, right click the mouse and click Add to enter datapoint setting box
Variable Name: Set the name of datapoint, for example, temp
Variable key, which can be filled in arbitrarily. The identifier cannot be repeated. For example, the MQTT identifier of the temperature data point is filled in as temp.
Select the acquisition address and choose data format according to the requirements. OCT/DEC/HEX are octal/decimal/hexadecimal respectively. The collected Modbus protocol address is input in decimal, so the example selects decimal.
Slave ID: The Modbus ID of the S475 device is "1", so fill in "1".
Address Type: S475 temperature supports function code 04, thus select 04 input register
Address: 24 (Datapoint temperature register address in S475 is 24)
Data Type: S475 temperature is 16-bit signed numeric data, thus select int16
Add Number: If consecutive addresses to be collected, the same function code can collect it simultaneously.
Read/Write: Automatic Identifying it according to Address Type
Ratio: Set the ratio to be multiplied or minified for uploading to cloud
Map Address: Modbus mapping address can be any from 0 to 2000 and it can’t be repeated. The outside of the mapping address on the configuration software represents the Modbus address, and M.XXX represents the PLC Modbus address.
Variable unit: Fill in according to requirements, or not fill in.
IEC Address Type(Uplink): Select the type of data to convert to IEC 104 format, such as convert the temperature data from S475 to IEC104 telecontrol data.
IOA: Storage Address for IEC 104 Data: For example, IOA address for "temp" is "16385"
Scale Factor: This field is used when uploading scaled telemetry data. The value must be less than 1. If set to the default value "0", the system will upload data as short floating point telemetry instead. Only 16-bit signed data from Modbus protocol is valid for this setting.For example, if the value is set to 0.01, the data will be uploaded as scaled telemetry, not as short floating point telemetry.
Maximum/Minimum: This field is used when uploading normalized telemetry (M_ME_NA_1) data. It defines the range of the collected data, which will be linearly mapped to the normalized range (-1, 1). The maximum and minimum values must both be set. The minimum value must be less than -1. If both values are set to the default "0", the data will be uploaded as short floating point telemetry (M_ME_NC_1) instead. This setting is only valid for 32-bit floating point data from the Modbus protocol.
Click OK to confirm.
Note: After confirming the configuration, datapoints will appear in the box like above picture. To add more datapoints, right click the box and click Add to enter configuration box, repeat Step (2)-(18)
Note: Click Write Configuration. Gateway will restart automatically. After device restarting, S475 datapoint is added successfully.

Add Mitsubishi PLC to Ethernet Port
Supports acquisition of Mitsubishi Q series (Q03UDE, Q04UDEH, Q06UDEH, Q10UDEH, Q13UDEH, Q20UDEH, Q26UDEH, Q002UD), L series (L02, L26-BT), FX5U series, IQ-L series (L04HCPU, L08HCPU, L16HCPU, L32HCPU).
Both the WAN port and the LAN port can collect the Mitsubishi PLC, which can be directly connected to the Mitsubishi PLC or collected through the switch. The configuration principle of the WAN port and the LAN port is the same. WAN port or LAN port acquisition configuration parameters of Mitsubishi Q/L series or FX5U should be consistent with the settings on the PLC.
Parameter settings on FX5U
For example, the parameter setting on Q06UDEH of Q series, Q/L series should select MC protocol communication.
WAN Port Configuration
WAN port collect data from FX5U through the switch, and the switch is connected to the external network. The configuration of the WAN port is as follows:
(1) Double-click "WAN" to pop up the WAN port configuration box.
(2) Auto IP: Whether the WAN port is enabled to obtain IP automatically, it is enabled by default, and can be set as required. In this example, the switch is connected to a router, and the router is enabled to automatically assign IP, so keep it enabled.
(3) IP address: The gateway obtains the IP address from the router. If it is a designated IP, set it according to the requirements, the PLC and the gateway should be in the same local area network.
(4) Subnet mask, the subnet mask of the WAN port gateway, if it is a designated IP, set it according to the requirements.
(5) Gateway: The gateway address obtained from the router. If you specify an IP, set it according to your needs.
(6) MAC address: The MAC address of the gateway.
(7) DNS: The DNS obtained by the gateway from the route, if it is a specified IP, set it according to the requirements.
(8) Click "OK".
Note: Click "Write Configuration" to restart the gateway automatically, and the configuration of the WAN port will not be changed until the restart.
Note: The IP address of the WAN port is the IP address that specifies which network segment the WAN port device is. If the IP address of the WAN port device is not the IP of the network segment set by the WAN, the WAN port cannot be collected. It is necessary to change the WAN port IP or change the WAN port according to the needs. The IP address of the port device. After changing the IP address of the gateway, it must be written into the configuration, and it will take effect after power off and restart.
Add Mitsubishi FX5U to WAN Port
(1) Click "WAN", click the right mouse button, click "Add" to enter device configuration box.
(2) Device name: Name the device, for example, because Mitsubishi FX5U is an example, you can fill in FX5U.
(3) Fill in the IP of the acquisition device, because the designated IP of FX5U is changed to: 192.168.1.112, so fill in 192.168.1.112 here. The IP is viewed on the Mitsubishi programming software. Fill in the IP of the PLC here. It should be noted that the IP of the PLC should be in the same network segment as the IP of the WAN port.
(4) Device port : Fill in as required. The example is 4999 from the Mitsubishi programming software, so it is 4999.
(5) Because it is collecting Mitsubishi FX5U, therefore, equipment brand: select Mitsubishi, equipment model: according to the communication data code setting on the Mitsubishi programming software is binary or ASCII code communication, binary select FX5U_BINARYMODE_ETH, ASCII code select FX5U_ASCIIMODE_ETH. The polling interval and communication timeout can be defaulted and filled in as required.
(6) Click "OK" to add FX5U.
Note: After clicking OK, the added device will be displayed under the WAN port, as shown in the figure above. If you want to add multiple devices, repeat steps (1)-(6).
Note: Click "Write Configuration" and the gateway will restart automatically. After restarting, the FX5U added to the WAN port will be added successfully.
Add Mitsubishi FX5U Datapoints
Configuration of data collected in register Y of FX5U is as follows:
(1) Click "FX5U", move the mouse cursor to the box, right-click the mouse, and click "Add" to enter data point setting box.
(2) Variable name: Name the data point, collecting the data of "Y7", you can fill in: "Y7".
(3) The identifier of the data point can be filled in arbitrarily. The identifier cannot be repeated, for example, the identifier of the collected "Y7" data point is filled in as "Y7".
(4)Select the acquisition address fill in the input gateway in what data format according to the requirements. OCT/DEC/HEX are octal/decimal/hexadecimal respectively. Mitsubishi FX5U X and Y data points are octal, so choose octal. Select according to the needs, such as register W is hexadecimal, register D is decimal.
(5) Address type: Selected according to the collected PLC data points. Collect the data of "Y7", select "Y".
(6) Address: the register address of the collected data point, Collecting the data of "Y7", the address is: 7.
(7) Data type: According to the type of register selected by PLC.
(8) Add Number: If it is to collect continuous addresses, the same register can be collected multiple times.
(9) Read/Write: Choose from "read only", "read and write".
(10) Ratio: Set the ratio to be multiplied or minified for uploading to cloud
(11) Map address: Set address where datapoint will be saved in gateway. Modbus mapping address can be any from 0 to 2000 and it can't be repeated.
(12) Variable unit: Fill in arbitrarily according to requirements, or not fill in.
(13) IEC Address Type(Uplink): Select the type of data to convert to IEC104 format, sch as convert the Y7 data to IEC104 telecontrol data.
(14)IOA: Storage Address for IEC 104 Data: For example, IOA address for "Y7" is "24584"
(15) Click "OK".
Note: After clicking "OK", the data points will be displayed in the box as shown in the figure above. If you want to continue adding data points, right-click on the box and click "Add" to enter data point configuration box, repeat (2)--(15) Steps.
Note: Clicking "Write Configuration" will restart the gateway automatically, and the data points collected from FX5U will take effect only after restarting.
Collecting OMRON PLC Data
Add OMRON PLC to COM Port
Select the COM port according to the serial port board, because the gateway device 485 interface is 2-wire, if the serial port board is 485, pay attention to the serial port board DIP switch to select 2-wire or 4-wire.
COM Port Configuration
Takes the serial port board CP1W-CIF11 and Omron CP1L-L as examples, the serial port board DIP switches 2 and 3 are ON, the DIP switch SW4 of CP1L-L is OFF, the serial port board and other DIP switches of the PLC The location is set as required. The serial port board RDA- or SDA- is connected to the gateway COM2 B, and RDB+or SDB+the gateway COM2 A. CP1L-L serial port configuration mode should select Host Link.
COM2 Configuration as shown
(1) Double-click "COM2" to enter COM configuration box.
(2) Mode selection: Collection.
(3) Since the example collection is Omron CP1L-L, the equipment brand: select "OMRON" from the drop-down box, and the equipment model: CJ/CS/CP. The polling interval and communication timeout are set according to requirements.
(4) The baud rate, stop bit, data bit, and parity bit are configured according to the parameters of the Omron CP1L-L serial port, which are consistent with them. Viewed from the Omron programming software, the Omron serial port selects the standard baud rate: 9600, stop bit: 2, data bit: 7, parity bit: Even.
(5) Click "OK".
Note: Click "Write Configuration" and the gateway device will restart automatically, and the configuration of the COM port will take effect after restarting.
Add CP1L to COM Port
(1) Click "COM2", click the right mouse button, click "Add" to enter device configuration box.
(2) Fill in the device name arbitrarily, such as: CP1L-L.
(3) Device address: CP1L-L serial port unit number, fill in as required, the address must be consistent with the unit number set by CP1L-L, otherwise communication will fail.
(4) Click "OK" to add the CP1L-L device.
Note: After clicking OK, the added devices will be displayed under COM2, as shown in the figure above, CP1L-L. If you want to add multiple devices, repeat steps (1)-(4).
Note: Click "Write Configuration" to restart the gateway device automatically. After restarting, the CP1L-L device with COM2 port is added successfully.
Add CP1L Datapoints
Take the CIO register of CP1L as an example
(1) Click "CP1L-L", move the mouse cursor to the box, right-click the mouse, and click "Add" to enter data point setting box.
(2) Variable name: Name the data point, such as: CIO2.01.
(3) The identifier of the data point can be filled in arbitrarily. The identifier cannot be repeated. For example, the identifier of the CIO2.01 data point is filled in as CIO201. Some platform identifiers cannot recognize the decimal point.
(4) Select the acquisition address fill in the input gateway in what data format according to the requirements. OCT/DEC/HEX are octal/decimal/hexadecimal respectively. The CIO register is collected in bits with decimals, so the decimal system is selected.
(5) Address type: Select according to Omron's register, if you want to collect "CIO2.01", select "CIO_BIT".
(6) Address: The register address of the collected data point, collecting "CIO2.01", so fill in "2.01".
(7) Data type: Select according to the acquisition PLC register, such as: "CIO_BIT" is the coil type, so select "bool".
(8) Add Number: The number of acquisitions. If it is to acquire continuous addresses, the same register can be acquired multiple times.
(9) Read/Write: according to PLC register selection. Select from "Read Only", "Read and Write".
(10) Mapping address: Enter the address where the collected data point will be stored in the gateway. You can assign it freely, but the mapping address must not be duplicated. Valid range: 0–2000.
For example, data collected from CIO2.01 is stored at register address “26” in the gateway.
In the configuration software, the mapping address represents the Modbus address, while the value in parentheses (M.XXX) represents the PLC Modbus address.
(11) Variable unit: fill in arbitrarily according to requirements, or not fill in.
(12) IEC Address Type(Uplink): Select the type of data to convert to IEC 104 format, sch as convert the CIO2.01 data to IEC104 telesignals data.
(13) IOA: Storage Address for IEC104 Data: For example, IOA address for "CIO2.01" is "9"
(14) Click "OK".
Note: After clicking "OK", the data points will be displayed in the box as shown in the figure above. If you want to continue adding data points, right-click on the box and click "Add" to enter data point configuration box, repeat (2)--(14) Steps.
Note: Clicking "Write Configuration" will restart the gateway automatically, and the data points added by CP1L-L will take effect only after restarting.

MAC Address: Input LAN port MAC address
Click OK to confirm it
Note: Click Write Configuration and Gateway will restart. Turn off the power of Gateway and restart it. After that LAN port configuration is done successfully
Note: LAN Port IP Address specifies the IP address arrange of LAN port device. If device IP address is not within the range, data can’t be collected. Thus it’s necessary to change LAN port IP address according to requirement. IP Address change will not be effective until gateway is powered off and powered on again.
Add OMRON PLC CP1L-EL to LAN Port
Click LAN and right click mouse and click Add to enter device configuration box
Device Name: set device name, for example, set CP1L-EL as device name.
Device IP: input PLC IP address. For example, PLC CP1L-EL IP is 192.168.3.151, thus put 192.168.3.151 here. This is PLC IP address. PLC IP address and LAN Port IP address must be in the same range.
Device Port: Fill in LAN port device port. CP1L-EL default port is 9600
Device Brand: Select Omron as Device Brand and select FINS_TCP as device model The polling interval and timeout are set according to requirements.
Click OK to confirm adding PLC CP1L-EL
Note: CP1L-EL device icon will appear after confirming the configuration. If more devices to be added, repeat Step (1)-(6)
Note: Click Write Configuration and gateway will restart automatically. After restarting, PLC CP1L-EL is added successfully
Add CP1L-EL Datapoints
Below example is part of PLC CP1L-EL register CIO & D data configuration
Click CP1L-EL, move mouse cursor to the right box, right click the mouse and click Add to enter datapoint configuration box
Variable Name: Set the name of datapoint, for example, D100
Variable key, which can be filled in arbitrarily. The identifier cannot be repeated.For example: D100
Select the collection address data format filled in the input gateway according to the requirements. OCT/DEC/HEX are octal/decimal/hexadecimal respectively. CP1L-EL D register is decimal, so choose decimal
Address Type: select address type according to PLC register. Here D100 address type is D
Address: Register address of datapoint. Here D100 address is 100
Data Type: select data type according to PLC register type
Add Number: If addresses are consecutive, the same register will collect multiple addresses.
Read/Write: Select from Read only and Read & Write.
Ratio: Set the ratio to be multiplied or minified for uploading to cloud
Map Address: Set address where datapoint will be saved in gateway.
Modbus mapping address can be any from 0 to 2000 and it can’t be repeated.
For example, if the mapping address of “D100” is set to “1”, the number outside represents the Modbus address in the configuration software, and the value in parentheses (M.XXX) represents the PLC Modbus address.
Variable unit: Fill in according to requirements, or not fill in.
IEC Address Type(Uplink): Select the type of data to convert to IEC104 format, sch as convert the CIO data to IEC104 telesignals data, area D data to IEC104 telemetry data.
IOA: Storage Address for IEC 104 Data: For example, IOA address for "D100" is "16386"
Click OK to confirm.
Note: After confirming the configuration, datapoints will appear in the box like above picture. To add more datapoints, right click the box and click Add to enter configuration box, repeat Step (2)-(15)
Note: Click Write Configuration. Gateway will restart automatically and CP1L-EL datapoint is added successfully.
Collecting Delta PLC Data
Add Delta PLC to COM Port
The configuration settings for all COM ports are the same. COM1–COM8 are selectable RS232/RS485 serial ports (default: RS485).
Since both COM2 and COM3 of the DVP-12SA2 are RS485 interfaces, COM2 of the gateway is used as an example to illustrate the COM port data acquisition configuration.
Connect DVP-12SA2 COM3+ to gateway COM2 A, and COM3– to gateway COM2 B.
COM Port Configuration
The configuration parameters for the DVP-12SA2 COM3 are as follows:
Configure the COM2 port according to the configuration parameters of DVP-12SA2 COM3
(1) Double-click "COM2" to enter COM setting box.
(2) Mode selection: Collection.
(3) Because it is a collection of Delta DVP-12SA2, the device brand: select DELTA from the drop-down box, and the device model: DELTA_DVP. The polling interval and communication timeout in the button are set according to requirements.
(4) The baud rate, stop bit, data bit and parity bit are configured according to the parameters of the COM3 port of DVP-12SA2, which are consistent with them. DVP-12SA2 COM3 port parameters Baud rate: 9600, stop bit: 1, data bit: 7, parity bit: Even.
(5) Click "OK".
Note: Click "Write Configuration" and the gateway device will restart automatically, and the configuration of the COM port will take effect after restarting.
Add DVP-12SA2 to COM Port
(1) Click "COM2", click the right mouse button, click "Add", to enter device setting box.
(2) Fill in the device name arbitrarily, such as: DVP-12SA2.
(3) Device address: The station number of Delta COM, fill in as required, the address must be consistent with the Delta COM setting, otherwise communication will fail.
(4) Click "OK" to add DVP-12SA2 device.
Note: After clicking OK, the added devices will be displayed under COM2, as shown in the figure above. DVP-12SA2, if you want to add multiple devices, repeat steps (1)-(4).
Note: Clicking "Write Configuration" will restart the gateway device automatically. After restarting, the DVP-12SA2 device with COM2 port added will be added successfully.
Add DVP-12SA2 Datapoints
Taking adding data points Y0 and D0 as an example, the address of register X and Y of Delta DVP-12SA2 is octal, and the address of register D is decimal.
(1) Click "DVP-12SA2", move the mouse cursor to the box, right-click the mouse, and click "Add" to enter data point setting box.
(2) Variable name: Name the data point, such as: Y0.
(3) The identifier of the data point can be filled in arbitrarily. The identifier cannot be repeated, for example, the identifier of the Y0 data point is filled in as Y0.
(4) Select the collection address data format filled in the input gateway according to the requirements. OCT/DEC/HEX are octal/decimal/hexadecimal respectively. Register X and Y addresses are in octal, and register D addresses are in decimal.
(5) Address type: According to Delta's register selection, if you want to collect "YO", select "Y".
(6) Address: The register address of the collected data point, such as: collecting "Y0", so fill in "0".
(7) Data type: Select according to the acquisition PLC register, such as: "Y0" is the coil type, so select "bool".
(8) Add Number: The number of acquisitions. If it is to acquire continuous addresses, the same register can be acquired multiple times.
(9) Read/Write: According to PLC register selection. Select from "Read Only", "Read and Write".
(10) Map address: Fill in the address where the collected data points are stored to the gateway, which can be filled in at will. Mapped addresses cannot be repeated. Range: 0-2000.
(11) Variable unit: Fill in arbitrarily according to requirements, or not fill in.
(12) IEC Address Type(Uplink): Select the type of data to convert to IEC 104 format, sch as convert the Y0 data to IEC104 telecontrol data, and DO data to IEC104 telemetry data.
(13) IOA: Storage Address for IEC 104 Data: For example, IOA address for "Y0" is "24585"
(14) Click "OK".
Note: After clicking "OK", the data points will be displayed in the box as shown in the figure above. If you want to continue adding data points, right-click on the box and click "Add" to enter data point configuration box, repeat (2)--(14) Steps.
Note: Clicking "Write Configuration" will restart the gateway automatically, and the data points added by DVP-12SA2 will take effect only after restarting.
Data Upload to IEC104 Platform
4.4.1 Configuration of IEC104 for the Gateway
In this example, COM1 port is used to collect Modbus RTU devices. The configuration for collecting devices can be referred to in section 5.2.2 for COM port configuration. The data points to be collected are as follows:
01 function code starts from 0, and two data points are converted to IEC104 telecontrol; 02 function code starts from 0, and two data points are converted to IEC104 telesignals; 03 function code starts from 0, and two data points are converted to IEC104 telemetry.
For uplink communication, taking the LAN-based IEC104 as an example, the data format for IEC104 on the cloud platform is the same.
Operation steps: (1) Double-click on "IEC104". (2) Click the enable button to enable the IEC104 server. Default: Disabled. Gray indicates: Disable, Green indicates: Enable. (3) Port: IEC104 server port number, default: 2404. (4) Click "Add" to set the server's IEC104 name and public address. (5) Add the IEC104 data points to be uploaded. Note: Telesignals, Telemetry, and Telecontrol should be added to their respective areas. Do not mix data points from different areas. The IOA of the added IEC104 data points should be consecutive. (6) Click "OK" to confirm the configuration of IEC104. (7) Click "Write Configuration", the gateway device will restart, and IEC104 will be enabled after the restart.
4.4.2 View and Send Data Via IEC870-5-104 Simulator
Using IEC870-5-104 Simulator to simulate the IEC104 master system for collecting data from the gateway. The IP address should be the WAN port IP address of the gateway. The port number should be the port number configured for IEC104 on the gateway, which is typically 2404 by default.
Set the public address for IEC104 and add the data points collected by IEC104 to match the settings on the gateway.
The data viewed on the IEC870-5-104 Simulator is as follows:
IEC870-5-104 Simulator: Sending telecontrol data:

Version History
Overview
Building HVAC Edge Gateway BA116 Series is an easy-to-use, high-performance building HVAC IoT gateway. It is equipped with a dual-core Cortex A7 processor, and can quickly collect data from up to 10,000 data points.
The BA116 Series adopts a building HVAC-grade design, with an onboard independent hardware watchdog and electrical isolation technology. It has passed EMC, EMI tests and has been validated for high and low-temperature performance in the range of -40°C to 85°C, ensuring stable long-term operation in harsh environments.
BA116 Series provides 4/8 isolated RS485/232 serial ports, supports WiFi, 4G network communications, routing functionality, OpenVPN support, TLS/SSL encryption, and remote firmware upgrade functionality.
BA116 Series can achieve remote PLC program downloads, PLC data acquisition, instrumentdata collection,and integration with various cloud platforms and digital platforms, providing reliable solutions for smart building HVAC and refrigeration (HVAC) systems. It is widely used in smart buildings, HVAC and refrigeration solutions, and digital transformation scenarios.
Key Features:
Powerful Performance:
Dual-core Cortex-A7 processor with up to 1.2GHz clock speed
High-speed acquisition of 10,000 data points; collects 1,000 data points in just 1.5 seconds
Independent hardware watchdog + EMC/EMI anti-interference design for long-term stable operation
512MB RAM and 8GB eMMC
Supports expandable SD card to enhance historical data storage performance.
Integrated routing + data acquisition + protocol conversion for high system integration
Industrial-Grade Reliability
Wide operating temperature range from -40°C to 85°C, adaptable to extreme environments
Titanium metal casing, corrosion-resistant and anti-electromagnetic interference
Electrical isolation + TLS/SSL encryption to ensure data security
Comprehensive Protocol Compatibility for Seamless Connectivity
Downlink: Siemens, Mitsubishi, Omron, Delta and other brands of PLCs, Modbus RTU, Modbus TCP, DL/T645, IEC104, BACnet/IP, BACnet MS/TP protocols, etc.
Uplink: BACnet/IP, BACnet MS/TP, MQTT, OPC UA, Huawei Cloud IoT, AWS IoT Core, Alibaba Cloud IoT, Modbus RTU, Modbus TCP, Thingsboard, BEILAI Cloud, Ignition SCADA, etc.
Smart O&M, Efficient Management
Remote PLC program downloads to reduce on-site maintenance
OpenVPN + secure encryption to ensure safe remote communication
Remote parameter configuration and firmware upgrades to improve efficiency
Flexible Communication Options
Communication Ports: 4/8 isolated RS485/232
Dual Ethernet ports + WiFi/4G wireless communication
Typical Application Areas
Smart Buildings: PLC Data Acquisition + MES/SCADA System Integration
Energy Management: Aggregation of data from electricity meters, water meters, and sensors to the cloud
Smart HVAC: Distributed Device Monitoring and Remote Maintenance

Product Introduction
Overview
BA116 series Building HVAC Protocol Gateways performs as industrial protocol converters, the gateways support IEC104, DL/T645, Siemens PLC, Mitsubishi PLC, Omron PLC, Delta PLC, BACnet/IP, BACnet MS/TP, Modbus RTU , Modbus TCP to BACnet/IP, BACnet MS/TP, OPC UA, Modbus RTU , Modbus TCP, MQTT, Huawei Cloud IoT, AWS IoT, Ali Cloud IoT, ThingsBoard, the gateways with Ethernet, 4G, WiFi, and RS485 support, it offers versatile connectivity for diverse building management applications.
Packing List
Before connecting IoT gateway, please make sure below items are included in the package:
(Pictures are for reference only.)
1 X IoT Gateway with terminal Blocks
4G or WiFi antenna(Optional)
1 x User Manual(PDF File Download Guide)
1 x SIM Card Picking PIN(For 4G version)
1 x Product Qualification Certificate
1 x Warranty Card
Note: If any of above items are missing, please contact BLIIOT Sales team.
Features
Downlink supports: IEC104, DL/T645, Siemens PLC, Mitsubishi PLC, Omron PLC, Delta PLC, BACnet/IP, BACnet MS/TP, Modbus RTU Master, Modbus TCP Master.
Uplink supports: BACnet/IP, BACnet MS/TP, OPC UA, Modbus RTU, Modbus TCP, MQTT, Huawei Cloud IoT, AWS IoT, Ali Cloud IoT, ThingsBoard.
DC 12-24V power supply with terminal wiring. Power input with reverse wiring prevention protection.
4CH or 8CH RS485/RS232.
Serial port baud rate supports 2400bps-115200bps; Stop bit supports 1, 2; Data bit supports 7, 8; Parity bit supports None, Odd, Even.
2 RJ45 Ethernet ports, 1WAN+1LAN. Data can be collected directly from devices or from devices on cascade switches via WAN and LAN ports. It comes with link and data indicators. Built-in isolation transformer for up to 2KV electromagnetic insulation.
Support routing function.
Support PLC remote debugging upload and download.
Support to view the status information of CPU usage, memory, network port device, serial port device, etc.
Support BLRMS for remote configuration, remote firmware upgrade for easy remote maintenance.
Supports Modbus RTU to Modbus TCP, transparent transmission.
Use RESET button to restore factory settings (long press in power-on state until RUN indicator goes out).
Support hardware and software watchdogs.
Support scheduled restart.
Aluminum alloy enclosure material, IP30 protection.
Dimension 46mmx83mmx110mm
DIN rail mounting or wall mounting.
Technical Parameter
Model Selection
If Wi-Fi is required, add "W" after the model, for example: BA116W
If 4G/5G is required, add "L" after the model, for example: BA116L
If both 4G and GPS versions are required, add "LG" after the model, for example: BA116LG
A single device supports up to 50 connected devices and 10,000 data points.
Hardware Introduction
Outline Dimension
Power Input
1 channels of 12~24VDC power input with reverse connection protection
DC V+ is positive, V- is negative, and PGND is ground.
SIM Card
When inserting/removing a SIM card, make sure the device is turned off.
Make sure device is placed flatly like above picture when inserting or removing SIM card.
Debugging
DEBUG is program debugging port.
4G and GPS Antenna
ANT1: 4G/Wi-Fi antenna port
ANT2: GPS antenna port
LED Indicator
When the gateway is operating, the LINK indicator must be blinking or steady on. Do not operate the gateway when the LINK indicator is off. Continuous operation with the LINK indicator off may damage the gateway.
Reset
After the gateway operates normally, press and hold the Reset button continuously for about 10 seconds until the RUN indicator goes out, at which time the gateway restores the factory default settings if it restarts automatically.
COM Port
WAN Port and LAN Port
ETH1 is the LAN port, and ETH2 is the WAN port. The WAN port is set to obtain an IP address automatically by default, while the LAN port has a default IP address of 192.168.3.1.
Mounting
The gateway supports wall-mounting and DIN-rail mounting.
Configuration
Login to Configuration Software
1. Connecting via the WAN port:
Use a standard crossover or straight-through Ethernet cable to connect the WAN port to a router or switch.
The router or switch gateway must not be 192.168.3.1.
The WAN and LAN ports cannot be on the same subnet.
Ensure that the gateway and your computer are on the same subnet.
Direct connection to a computer:
Use a standard crossover Ethernet cable to connect the LAN port of the gateway directly to the computer.
When connecting directly, the computer must be assigned a static IP within the 192.168.3.1 range (since the LAN port default IP is 192.168.3.1).
The computer’s static IP configuration must include the IP address, subnet mask, gateway, and DNS.
Note: The WAN port is factory-set to obtain an IP address automatically, and the LAN port is factory-set with the default IP address 192.168.3.1.
Open Configuration Software
Search for IoT Gateway
Click "Search" and all devices in the same local area network with the PC will appear. For example, WAN port is connected to the switch, PC and gateway are in the same local area network, and the gateway whose IP is 192.168.1.173 will be found. If there is no device found, please make sure gateway and computer is in the same local area network, and the computer UDP broadcast is normal. If the device cannot be found because of the computer network environment issues, you can enter the IP in the "IP" bar, click connect, login.
Changing the computer IP or gateway requires closing the configuration software and reopening it. Changing the gateway on the IoT gateway's network port also requires closing the configuration software and reopening it.
Connecting to IoT Gateway
Select the gateway device you want to configure, double click, (Example: double click on the IoT gateway with IP: 192.168.1.173), and enter the IoT gateway configuration interface. No display device is available due to the network environment of the computer, you can enter the IP to log in.
Default password: 123456
Configuration Software Introduction
System Function
Advanced Settings
The private network setting is to allow a private Ethernet or 4G network to set the IP that can be used or the server that can be connected. If it is a normal Ethernet or 4G network, there is no need to set it.
COM Port
All 8 COM Port configuration is the same. Take the COM1 configuration as an example.
COM Port Attribute Configuration
Double click COM1 to open COM Port Attribute configuration box.
Add COM Port Device
Right click COM1 and click Add to add device. Device configuration box will pop up. For the added device, double click it to show device configuration information. Right click to delete device.
The byte order of the configuration data points is also set here
Note: Device attributes are set according to the selected protocol. For example, device brand is Modbus, set attributes as below.
Add COM Port Device Datapoints
Click device name and then right click the box on the right, then click Add to configure data points. The M.XXX in the brackets of the mapping address on the configuration software represents the PLC Modbus address, and the value outside of the brackets represents the Modbus address.
Right click "Add" to add the next data point. You can also right click to delete the data, or double click the data point to edit the data.
You can add data points by importing and exporting Excel file. First, create some data points to export, the configuration content of the Excel file is the same as the information configuration principle of the data point configuration box. The variable name, variable label, mapping address, and collection address can not conflict.

LAN Port Attribute Configuration
Double click LAN port to enter setting page. Factory default IP of LAN is 192.168.3.1. Auto IP address distribution and routing functions are disabled by default. Note: If the LAN port is connected to a switch, make sure that no cables from other network segments are connected to the switch.

Double click WAN to enter WAN Port Attribute configuration box.

The procedure to add WAN Port device datapoint is the same as that of adding COM port device data point. ID of the Modbus TCP device is configured in the data point configuration box.
