Data Service
Transparent Transmission
Set COM mode to Pass Through, set COM parameters and then configure Transparent Transmission parameters. All 8 COM ports can be used for Transparent Transmission. The configuration procedures are the same. Only one COM port can be used for transparent transmission at a time; 8 COM ports cannot be used for transparent transmission simultaneously. Below is the example of setting COM2 for transparent transmission: 1. select Pass Through mode in COM2, 2. configure COM2 attributes, click OK to enter Data Service configuration page.
Modbus RTU to Modbus TCP
To configure Modbus RTU to Modbus TCP protocol, you need to set the COM mode as "Modbus RTU to Modbus TCP" and set the COM parameters, and then configure the parameters of Modbus RTU to Modbus TCP protocol. Modbus RTU to Modbus TCP protocol can be selected for all 8 COM ports, but 8 COM ports cannot be supported at the same time, only one COM port can be selected.
Below is the example of setting COM2 as Modbus RTU to TCP: 1. Select Modbus RTU to Modbus TCP mode, 2. Set COM port parameters. 3. Click OK to enter Data Service for configuring Modbus RTU to Modbus TCP.
Modbus TCP Server
The gateway device supports the Modbus TCP protocol and provides data externally as a Modbus TCP server. The Modbus TCP server is always enabled; you only need to configure the local listening port. The IP address of the Modbus TCP server can be selected based on the need for WAN or LAN. Click "WAN" or "LAN" to view the IP addresses of WAN and LAN.
The Modbus TCP host acts as a client, collecting gateway data supporting function codes: Boolean values support "01" and "05", numerical values support "03" and "06". For 16-bit byte order, it's AB; for 32-bit byte order, it's ABCD. When configuring the collection address, use either the Modbus address if referring to the host's Modbus address, or PLC Modbus address (with addresses mapped outside indicating Modbus addresses, and those in parentheses like M.XXX indicating PLC Modbus addresses). Specific addresses can be found on the data point page as shown below. Configuration of the host can refer to section 5.5.2 View and Send Command with KingView.
Modbus RTU Slave
The gateway device supports providing data externally as a Modbus slave. Select "Modbus RTU SLAVE" as the port, and set the local Slave ID and COM port parameters. Any of the 8 COM ports can be selected for Modbus RTU SLAVE.
When acting as a slave, the gateway supports the following function codes for collecting data: Boolean values support "01" and "05", while numerical values support "03" and "06". For 16-bit byte order, it's AB, and for 32-bit byte order, it's ABCD. When configuring the collection address, use either the Modbus address if referring to the host's Modbus address, or PLC Modbus address (with addresses mapped outside indicating Modbus addresses, and those in parentheses like M.XXX indicating PLC Modbus addresses). Specific addresses can be found on the data point page as shown below.
BACnet/IP
The BACnet standard is designed for heating, ventilation, air conditioning, and refrigeration control devices. It also provides a fundamental principle for the integration of other building control systems, such as lighting, security, fire safety, and more.
The gateway acts as a BACnet/IP server to provide data externally. Since the data attributes of various protocols differ, it provides data using the current values of four object properties: AI (Analog Input), AO (Analog Output), BI (Binary Input), and BO (Binary Output). The object instance corresponds to the Modbus address mapped in the data point page of the configuration software.
Note: Uplink and downlink BACnet/IP cannot be enabled simultaneously.
For configuring the BACnet/IP master system to collect data from the gateway, you can refer to using Yabe to simulate the BACnet/IP master, view and send data.
BACnet MS/TP
The gateway acts as a BACnet MS/TP server to provide data externally. Since the data attributes differ across various protocols, it provides data using the current values of four object properties: AI (Analog Input), AO (Analog Output), BI (Binary Input), and BO (Binary Output). The object instance corresponds to the Modbus address mapped in the data point page of the configuration software.
To ensure communication stability, the gateway's COM ports are equipped with RS485 terminal resistors.
OPC UA
The gateway device supports OPC UA, providing data externally as an OPC UA server. The IP address of the OPC UA server can be selected based on the need for WAN or LAN. Click "WAN" or "LAN" to view the IP addresses of WAN and LAN.
The configuration of the OPC UA client for collecting gateway data can be referenced using UaExpert to view and transmit data.
OPC UA client data points can be automatically generated directly from the gateway, and may not require manual input. The name of the data point is composed of the device name and the variable name of the device's data point in the configuration software. The Node ID is composed of the device name and the data point tag of the device in the configuration software.
Cloud Platform
BA116 series gateways can be online in multiple cloud platform simultaneously.
MQTT Client
MQTT Client can be connected to cloud with certificate or without certificate.
MQTT Client data format only supports JSON data format of "BLIIOT", "thingsboard", and "sparkplug b". MQTT data format can be customized. More JSON data format and customized JSON data format will be supported in the future.
Connect to the ThingsBoard platform, select JSON data format of "thingsboard-telemetry-gateway". ThingsBoard platform domain name is thingsboard.cloud.
Connect to a platform that supports Sparkplug B, such as the ignition, select the JSON data format of "sparkplug b", click the button next to the data template item, configure the group ID and edge node ID in the configuration box.
MQTT Client supports multiple publishing topics, click "Add" in the publishing topic item to fill in the publishing topic, and you can view the publishing topic name in the drop-down box of the publishing topic item. Select the release topic name and click "Delete" to delete the release topic to be deleted.
MQTT Client also supports the selection of different data points for each publishing topic to publish. Put the mouse cursor in the right box, click the right button, a prompt box will pop up, click "Add", a data point box will pop up, click the data point to be published, Click "OK". Double-click a data point to view the properties. As shown in the figure below: Only the data point “TAG001” from the device on ”COM1”, will be published under the topic “test”, while other data points will not be published.
The "BLIIOT" JSON data format of MQTT Client and MQTT Client II is the same as that of BLIIOT Cloud MQTT. Refer to BLIIOT MQTT Data Format.
"thingsboard-telemetry-gateway" JSON data format, publish and subscribe topic format refer to the thingsboard official website documentation.
"sparkplug b" JSON data format, publish and subscribe topic format refer to Sparkplug specification.
Note: The data point box is blank by default, if no data point is selected, all data points are published. If there are multiple publishing topics, only one publishing topic can be blank, and other topics must select the published data points, and cannot be left blank.
MQTT Client II
MQTT Client II Configuration is the same as MQTT Client
MQTT Client II configuration refer to MQTT Client
MQTT Client II subscribe topic will not be working. MQTT Client II is used for view data but not control data from cloud.
MQTT Client II and MQTT Client“BLIIOT”JSON data format is the same as that of BLIIOT MQTT. Refer to BLIIOT MQTT Data Format
Alibaba Cloud
Alibaba Cloud device model is under development. Thus datapoint must be added one by one. MQTT flag must be the same as the one in configuration software.
For example, When collecting the S7-200SMART data point VW8, the configured variable tag is “VW8”. Therefore, the identifier of the data point added on the Alibaba Cloud platform should also be “VW8”. The function name does not necessarily need to be the same as the variable name in the configuration software.
Note: The Device Shadow feature of Alibaba Cloud is not supported. To send data to the device, use the online debugging section. Multiple data transmissions are not supported.
HUAWEI Cloud
HUAWEI Cloud can be connected with or without Certificate.
It supports multiple service IDs. Click Add to set Service ID. ID can be viewed from the drop-down list. Click Delete to delete service ID.
HUAWEI Cloud supports uploading certain datapoints of each Service ID. Right click the box and click Add to enter datapoint dialog box. Select the datapoint to upload and click OK to confirm it. Double click the datapoint to view its attributes.
Note: 1. Datapoint box is blank in default which means all datapoints will be uploaded. If there’re multiple Service IDs, only one Service ID datapoint box can be blank. Datapoints for uploading must be selected for other Service IDs.
The Device Shadow feature of Huawei Cloud is not supported. Data can only be sent using the synchronous command sending function.
Set datapoint in HUAWEI Cloud as below picture. If there’re multiple service IDs in configuration software and each service ID has different datapoints, configure the same service ID in HUAWEI Cloud. Put MQTT flag as attribute name. For example, collect datapoint Q0 of PLC S7-200SMART, put configuration software MQTT flag Q0 as attribute name.
AWS
Note: 1. Datapoint box is blank in default which means all datapoints will be published. If multiple topics are published, only one topic datapoint box can be blank. For other topics, datapoints for publishing must be selected.
2.AWS Cloud data shadow function is not supported.
BLIIOT Cloud via MQTT
BLIIOT MQTT Data Format refer to: BLIIOT MQTT Data Format
For configuring data points on BLIIOT MQTT, you first need to add the data point, then go to the link protocol item to configure the data point identifier. The identifier of the data point should match the MQTT identifier in the configuration software. For example, if the IEC 104 single point remote signal data point is 10 and the MQTT identifier in the configuration software is "telesignals1", then the read/write identifier on the BLIIoT Cloud should be "telesignals1".
BLIIOT Cloud via Modbus
Both BLIIOT Cloud and custom Modbus cloud can be connected via Modbus RTU protocol. BA116 series gateway supports function code 01, 05 of Boolean data and function codes 03, 06 of numerical data. 16-bit byte sequence is AB and 32-bit byte sequence is ABCD.
The configuration for BLIIoT Modbus platform data points is as follows: First, create the data point, then go to the set link protocol item to configure the read/write instructions for the data point, including Modbus ID, function code, address, data format, byte order, and collection period. The Modbus address entered in BLIIoT Cloud should be offset by one compared to the Modbus mapped address in the configuration software. For example, if the IEC 104 single point remote signal data point is 10 and the Modbus mapped address in the configuration software is "0", then the offset entered in BLIIoT Cloud should be "1". The names of the sensors uploaded to the platform can be different from those configured in the software.
BLRMS
To use the BLRMS service, you need to set up the "BLRMS Management Platform" in the advanced settings.
BA116 Gateway Application Example
Collecting Metering Devices
Metering Collection via COM Port
The COM port currently only supports collecting data from electricity meters using the DL/T645-2007 protocol.
COM Port Configuration
Using COM to Collect Data from Electricity Meters using the DL/T645-2007 Protocol as an Example
(1) Double-click on "COM1".
(2) Select mode: Collection.
(3) Since it's for collecting data from electricity meters, select "DLT645" from the drop down menu for device brand, and "DL/T645-2007" for device model. Adjust the polling interval and communication timeout according to requirements. It's recommended to set the polling interval and timeout to over 2 seconds if the baud rate is low and there are many data points.
(4) Configure baud rate, stop bits, data bits, and parity according to the parameters set on the COM port of the electricity meter, ensuring consistency.
(5) Click "OK".
Note: Clicking "Write Configuration" will automatically restart the gateway device. After the restart, the configuration for COM1 port will take effect.
Adding Electricity Meters via COM Port
Click on "COM1", then right-click, and select "Add".
Enter any name for the device, for example, "DL/T645".
Enter the communication address, which corresponds to the address field or meter number of the electricity meter.
Click "OK" to add the electricity meter device.
Note: After clicking "OK", the added device will be displayed under COM1. If you want to add multiple devices, repeat steps (1) to (4).
Note: Clicking "Write Configuration" will automatically restart the gateway device. After the restart, the electricity meter devices added under COM1 will be successfully added.
Add Electricity Meters Datapoints
(1) Click on "DL/T645", move the mouse cursor to the box, right-click, and then click "Add".
(2) Variable Name: Name the data point of the meter, for example: "Phase A Voltage".
(3) The identifier of the data point can be filled arbitrarily, but it must be unique. For example, if collecting data for "Phase A Voltage", the MQTT identifier can be filled as "REG001".
(4) Choose the collection address format according to the requirements and fill in with OCT/DEC/HEX for octal/decimal/hexadecimal respectively. Do not fill in the meter collection address, select decimal.
(5) Address Type: Select the data point of the meter to be collected from the drop down menu. For example, if you want to collect data for "Phase A Voltage", then choose "Phase A Voltage".
(6) Address: Not applicable in the meter protocol, should not be filled in.
(7) Data Type: Data collected from the meter in the gateway is stored as 32-bit single-precision floating-point.
(8) Add Number: Not applicable in the meter protocol, should not be filled in.
(9) Read/Write Type: Read-only.
(10) Ratio: Specify how much to multiplying or reducing the data before uploading it to the platform, fill in according to requirements.
(11) Map Address: Specify the address where the data collected from the meter will be stored in the gateway. This can be filled arbitrarily, but mapping addresses must be unique. For example, if collecting data for "Phase A Voltage", the mapping address can be "0". In the configuration software, mapping addresses are represented as Modbus addresses outside the brackets, while inside the brackets, M.XXX represents the PLC Modbus address.
(12) Variable Unit: Fill in as needed, and it can be left blank.
(13) COV Precision: Set the change threshold to trigger COV notifications. This only needs to be configured when enabling the COV notification function on the uplink BACnet/IP.
(14) Click "OK".
Note: After clicking "OK", the data point will be displayed in the box as shown above. If you want to continue adding data points, right-click inside the box, click "Add" to open the data point configuration box, and repeat steps (2) to (14).
Note: Clicking "Write Configuration" will automatically restart the gateway. After restarting, the collected data points from the meter will take effect.
Add IEC104 Device to Ethernet Port
Both WAN and LAN ports can collect data from IEC104 devices, either by direct connection or through a switch. When collecting data from IEC104 devices, the device's data points cannot exceed 1000.
Multiple public addresses cannot be collected simultaneously. Supported functional types for data collection include: 01 Single-point telesignals input (M_SP_NA_1), 03 Double-point telesignals input (M_DP_NA_1), 13 Short floating-point telemetry input (M_ME_NC_1), 45 Single-point telecontrol (C_SC_NA_1), and 46 Double-point telecontrol (C_DC_NA_1).
WAN Port Configuration
This example demonstrates collecting IEC104 data via the WAN port through a switch, with the switch connected to the external network. The configuration for the WAN port is as follows:
(1) Double-click on "WAN".
(2) Auto IP: Specify whether the WAN port should automatically obtain an IP address. By default, this is enabled. Set according to your requirements. In this example, the switch is connected to a router that automatically assigns IP addresses, so keep this option enabled.
(3) IP Address: The gateway obtains its IP address from the router. If a specific IP address is required, set it accordingly. The IEC104 devices and the gateway should be on the same LAN.
(4) Subnet Mask: Specify the subnet mask for the WAN port of the gateway. If a specific IP address is required, set it accordingly.
(5) Gateway: The gateway obtains the gateway address from the router. If a specific IP address is required, set it accordingly.
(6) MAC Address: The MAC address of the gateway.
(7) DNS: The DNS obtained by the gateway from the router. If a specific IP address is required, set it accordingly.
(8) Click "OK".
Note: Clicking "Write Configuration" will automatically restart the gateway. After restarting, the configuration changes for the WAN port will take effect.
Attention: The IP address of the WAN port specifies which IP address segment the WAN port device belongs to. If the IP address of the WAN port device does not belong to the IP segment set for the WAN port, data collection via the WAN port will not work. You may need to change either the IP address of the WAN port or the IP address of the WAN port device according to your requirements. Changes to the gateway's IP address will take effect only after writing configuration and restarting the device.
Add IEC104 Device to WAN Port
(1) Click on "WAN", right-click, then click "Add".
(2) Device Name: Name the device, for example: "IEC104".
(3) Enter the IP address of the collection device. For example, if the IEC104 device’s IP is 192.168.1.158, fill in this address here. Note that the IEC104 device’s IP must be in the same subnet as the WAN port’s IP.
(4) Device Port: Fill in according to your requirements, for example: 2404.
(5) Since it is collecting IEC104 data, select "IEC" for Device Brand and "IEC104" for Device Model.
(6) Fill in the parameters for the IEC104 device, including polling intervals, K, W, T0, T1, T2, T3, according to your requirements, or leave them as default.
(7) Click "OK" to add the IEC104 device.
Note: After clicking "OK", the added device will be displayed under the WAN port. If you want to add multiple devices, repeat steps (1) to (7).
Clicking "Write Configuration" will automatically restart the gateway. After restarting, the IEC104 devices added under the WAN port will be successfully added.
Add IEC104 Device Datapoints
The Help section contains the English–Chinese correspondence for IEC104 data point function codes.
(1) Click on "IEC104", right-click, and then click "Add".
(2) Variable Name: Name the data point, for example: if collecting data for "Short Floating Point Telemetry 16385", it can be named as "M_ME_NC_1".
(3) The identifier of the data point can be filled arbitrarily, but it must be unique. For example, if collecting data for "Short Floating Point Telemetry 16385", the identifier can be filled as "REG004".
(4) Choose the collection address format according to the requirements and fill in the gateway with OCT/DEC/HEX for octal/decimal/hexadecimal respectively. For IEC104 data points, select decimal.
(5) Address Type: Select the type of IEC104 data to be collected, such as telesignals, telemetry, or telecontrol according to your requirements.
(6) Address: IEC104 information object address, for example: Collecting data for "16385" short floating point telemetry.
(7) Data Type: Select the data type based on the selected functional type of the IEC104 device.
(8) Add Number: If collecting continuous addresses, multiple collections can be made from the same register.
(9) Read/Write: Select from "Read Only" or "Read/Write" options.
(10) Map Address: Specify the address where the collected data point will be stored in the gateway device. This can be filled arbitrarily, but mapping addresses must be unique. For example, if collecting data for "Short Floating Point Telemetry 16385", the mapping address can be "20". In the configuration software, mapping addresses are represented as Modbus addresses outside the brackets, while inside the brackets, M.XXX represents the PLC Modbus address.
(11) Variable Unit: Fill in as needed, or leave it blank.
(12) COV Precision: Set the change threshold to trigger COV notifications. This field only needs to be filled when enabling the COV notification function on uplink BACnet/IP.
(13) Click "OK".
Note: After clicking "OK", the data point will be displayed in the box as shown above. If you want to continue adding data points, right-click inside the box, click "Add" to open the data point configuration box, and repeat steps (2) to (13).
Note: Clicking "Write Configuration" will automatically restart the gateway. After restarting, the collected IEC104 data points will take effect.
Add Modbus Device
For example, when using COM2 to collect DI and DO data from BEILAI’s M140T Industrial Ethernet Remote I/O Data Acquisition Module, and using the LAN port to collect data from the S475 Industrial IoT Controller, the COM2 port collects data via the Modbus TCP protocol, while the LAN port collects data via the Modbus TCP protocol.
The WAN port of the BA116 gateway is connected to the LAN port of the R40 router, which provides network access for the BA116 gateway.
Connect M140T & S475 to BA116
Network Switch is connected to BA116 LAN port. S475 is connected to Switch. S475 device data is collected through LAN via Modbus TCP. When the LAN port is connected to a switch, the other devices connected to the LAN port cannot be in a different subnet. M140T data is collected through COM2 via Modbus RTU protocol.
The collected data is transmitted via WAN port to a 4G router (R40). The R40 uses its routing function to upload the data to various cloud platforms.
Note: Both WAN and LAN can collect device data. The configuration procedure is the same. This example is introduction to LAN port configuration.
COM Port Configuration
The configuration method of 8 COM ports is the same. Take COM2 connection as an example.
COM2 Configuration
COM2 collect M140T data via Modbus RTU
Double click “COM2” to enter setting box
Mode Selection: Collection
Device Brand: Modbus; Device Model: Modbus RTU The polling interval and timeout are set by default and set according to requirements.
Baud rate, Stop bit, Data Bit and Parity Bit will be set the same as that in M140T RS485 port
Click OK to confirm
Note: Click Write Configuration. Gateway will restart automatically. COM configuration will be valid after device restarting
Add COM Port Device M140T
Click COM2, right click the mouse and click Add to enter configuration box
Set device name, for example, set M140T as device name
Input device Modbus adress, for example, if M140T Modbus ID is 1, input 1
Select Type of data to be collected. The example is to collect the DI and DO of the M140T, both of which are Boolean type, not numeric type register, select as default.
Write function code: As default, M140T supports writing multiple registers.
Click OK to confirm adding M140T
Click COM2 to view the added device M140T. If more devices to be added, repeat Step (1)-(6)
Note: Click Write Configuration. Gateway will restart automatically. After restarting, M140T is added successfully.
Add M140T Datapoints
Click M140T, move mouse cursor to the right box, right click mouse to enter datapoint setting box.
Variable name: Set datapoint name, for example, DO1
Variable key, which can be filled in arbitrarily. The identifier cannot be repeated, for example, the MQTT identifier of the DO1 data point is filled in as DO1.
Select the acquisition address and choose data format according to the requirements, OCT/DEC/HEX are octal/decimal/hexadecimal respectively. The collected Modbus protocol address is input in decimal, so the example selects decimal.
Address type: Select according to the function codes supported by the collected data points. For example, the DO of the collected M140T supports the "01" function code, so select "01 Coil Status", and DI supports the "02" function code, so select "02 Input Status”
Address: The register address of the data point, such as: data point DO1 is "0" register address in the M140T, so fill in "0".
Data type: Select according to the data point, such as: DI and DO of M140T are both coil types, so select "bool".
Add Number: If it is collecting continuous addresses, the same function code can be collected multiple times.
Read/Write: Automatic identifying read-write type according to Address Type
Map address: Fill in the address where the collected data points are stored to the BA116 gateway device, which can be filled in at will. Mapped addresses cannot be duplicated. For example, the data collected from DO1 is stored in the “0” register address of the gateway. The outside of the mapping address on the configuration software represents the Modbus address, and M.XXX represents the PLC Modbus address.
Variable Unit: Input any required unit or leave it blank.
COV Precision: Set the change threshold to trigger COV notifications. This field only needs to be filled in when the COV notification function is enabled for uplink BACnet/IP.
Click OK to confirm
Note: After clicking OK to confirm the configuration, datapoints will appear in the box lik above picture. If more datapoints to be added, right click the box and click Add to enter datapoint configuration box, repeat Step (2)-(13)
Note: Click Write Configuration. Gateway will restart automatically. After restarting, M140T datapoints are added successfully
Ethernet Port Configuration
Both WAN and LAN can collect device data. The configuration procedure is the same.
LAN Port Configuration
Below is the example of configuring LAN port to connect S475.
Double click LAN to enter setting box
DHCP: Enable auto IP distribution. Default is disabled. For examples, S475 has been set to auto retrieving IP, then LAN port must enable DHCP.
Routing: Enable network rounting function. Default is disabled. For example, S475 data will be collected without network requirement, then disable routing function
IP Address: defaut is 192.168.3.1, the IP address assigned to the LAN device must be within the same subnet. It can be changed according to requirement. For example, S475 is set to auto retrieving IP and the range is not limited, thus it’s not necessary to change it.
Subnet mask: The subnet mask of the LAN port gateway.
MAC Addres: Input LAN port MAC address
Click OK to confirm it
Note: Click Write Configuration and Gateway will restart. Turn off the power of Gateway and restart it. After that LAN port configuration is done successfully
Note: The IP address of the LAN port determines which subnet the LAN devices belong to. If the IP address of the LAN device does not match the subnet set for the LAN port, the LAN port will not be able to collect data. Depending on the requirement, either the LAN port's IP address or the LAN device's IP address should be changed. Any changes to the gateway's IP address must write configuration, and remove power to reboot device is required for the changes to take effect.
Add LAN Port Device S475
Click LAN and right click mouse to enter device setting box
Device Name: Such as S475
Device IP: For example, S475 is set to auto retrieving IP. Open S475 configuration software and view its IP(192.168.3.125). Thus input S475 IP 192.168.3.125.
Note: If LAN port IP is changed and LAN port device auto retrieves IP, please click Write Configuration, power off gateway and power it on again. Then IP change can be viewed
Device Port: LAN port device port. For example, S475 Modbus TCP port is 502.
Device Brand: Modbus; Device Model: Modbus TCP
(Gateway collects S475 through LAN port through Modbus TCP protocol)
The polling interval and timeout in the button can be defaulted and filled in according to requirements.
Select Data Type. For example, S475 power source and temperature & humidity data is 16-bit AB type, 32-bit data is not collected. Thus select 16-bit AB type and keep 32-bit data type with default setting
Write function code: choose 15/16, and choose according to the function code supported by the device.
Click OK to confirm the setting
Note: S475 device icon will appear after confirming the configuration. If more devices to be added, repeat Step (1)-(9)
Note: Click Write Configuration and gateway will restart automatically. After restarting, device S475 is added successfully
Add S475 Datapoints
Click S475, move mouse cursor to the right box, right click the mouse and click Add to enter datapoint setting box
Variable Name: Set the name of datapoint, for example, temp
Variable key, which can be filled in arbitrarily. The identifier cannot be repeated. For example, the MQTT identifier of the temperature data point is filled in as temp.
Select the acquisition address and choose data format according to the requirements. OCT/DEC/HEX are octal/decimal/hexadecimal respectively. The collected Modbus protocol address is input in decimal, so the example selects decimal.
Slave ID: The Modbus ID of the S475 device is "1", so fill in "1".
Address Type: S475 temperature supports function code 04, thus select 04 input register
Address: 24 (Datapoint temperature register address in S475 is 24)
Data Type: S475 temperature is 16-bit signed numeric data, thus select int16
Add Number: If consecutive addresses to be collected, the same function code can collect it simultaneously.
Read/Write: Automatic Identifying it according to Address Type
Ratio: Set the ratio to be multiplied or minified for uploading to cloud
Map Address: Enter the address where the collected data points will be stored in the gateway device. The address can be filled in freely, but mapping addresses must not be duplicated. The outside of the mapping address on the configuration software represents the Modbus address, and M.XXX represents the PLC Modbus address.
Variable unit: Fill in according to requirements, or not fill in.
COV Precision: Set the change threshold that triggers the COV notification. This field only needs to be filled in when the COV notification function is enabled on the uplink BACnet/IP.
Click OK to confirm.
Note: After confirming the configuration, datapoints will appear in the box like above picture. To add more datapoints, right click the box and click Add to enter configuration box, repeat Step (2)-(15)
Note: Click Write Configuration. Gateway will restart automatically. After device restarting, S475 datapoint is added successfully.

Add Mitsubishi PLC to Ethernet Port
Supports acquisition of Mitsubishi Q series (Q03UDE, Q04UDEH, Q06UDEH, Q10UDEH, Q13UDEH, Q20UDEH, Q26UDEH, Q002UD), L series (L02, L26-BT), FX5U series, IQ-L series (L04HCPU, L08HCPU, L16HCPU, L32HCPU).
Both the WAN port and the LAN port can collect the Mitsubishi PLC, which can be directly connected to the Mitsubishi PLC or collected through the switch. The configuration principle of the WAN port and the LAN port is the same. WAN port or LAN port acquisition configuration parameters of Mitsubishi Q/L series or FX5U should be consistent with the settings on the PLC.
Parameter settings on FX5U
For example, the parameter setting on Q06UDEH of Q series, Q/L series should select MC protocol communication.
WAN Port Configuration
WAN port collect data from FX5U through the switch, and the switch is connected to the external network. The configuration of the WAN port is as follows:
(1) Double-click "WAN" to pop up the WAN port configuration box.
(2) Auto IP: Whether the WAN port is enabled to obtain IP automatically, it is enabled by default, and can be set as required. In this example, the switch is connected to a router, and the router is enabled to automatically assign IP, so keep it enabled.
(3) IP address: The gateway obtains the IP address from the router. If it is a designated IP, set it according to the requirements, the PLC and the gateway should be in the same local area network.
(4) Subnet mask, the subnet mask of the WAN port gateway, if it is a designated IP, set it according to the requirements.
(5) Gateway: The gateway address obtained from the router. If you specify an IP, set it according to your needs.
(6) MAC address: The MAC address of the gateway.
(7) DNS: The DNS obtained by the gateway from the route, if it is a specified IP, set it according to the requirements.
(8) Click "OK".
Note: Click "Write Configuration" to restart the gateway automatically, and the configuration of the WAN port will not be changed until the restart.
Note: The IP address of the WAN port is the IP address that specifies which network segment the WAN port device is. If the IP address of the WAN port device is not the IP of the network segment set by the WAN, the WAN port cannot be collected. It is necessary to change the WAN port IP or change the WAN port according to the needs. The IP address of the port device. After changing the IP address of the gateway, it must be written into the configuration, and it will take effect after power off and restart.
Add Mitsubishi FX5U to WAN Port
(1) Click "WAN", click the right mouse button, click "Add" to enter device configuration box.
(2) Device name: Name the device, for example, because Mitsubishi FX5U is an example, you can fill in FX5U.
(3) Fill in the IP of the acquisition device, because the designated IP of FX5U is changed to: 192.168.1.112, so fill in 192.168.1.112 here. The IP is viewed on the Mitsubishi programming software. Fill in the IP of the PLC here. It should be noted that the IP of the PLC should be in the same network segment as the IP of the WAN port.
(4) Device port : Fill in as required. The example is 4999 from the Mitsubishi programming software, so it is 4999.
(5) Because it is collecting Mitsubishi FX5U, therefore, equipment brand: select Mitsubishi, equipment model: according to the communication data code setting on the Mitsubishi programming software is binary or ASCII code communication, binary select FX5U_BINARYMODE_ETH, ASCII code select FX5U_ASCIIMODE_ETH. The polling interval and communication timeout can be defaulted and filled in as required.
(6) Click "OK" to add FX5U.
Note: After clicking OK, the added device will be displayed under the WAN port, as shown in the figure above. If you want to add multiple devices, repeat steps (1)-(6).
Note: Click "Write Configuration" and the gateway will restart automatically. After restarting, the FX5U added to the WAN port will be added successfully.
Add Mitsubishi FX5U Datapoints
Configuration of data collected in register Y of FX5U is as follows:
(1) Click "FX5U", move the mouse cursor to the box, right-click the mouse, and click "Add" to enter data point setting box.
(2) Variable name: Name the data point, collecting the data of "Y7", you can fill in: "Y7".
(3) The identifier of the data point can be filled in arbitrarily. The identifier cannot be repeated, for example, the identifier of the collected "Y7" data point is filled in as "Y7".
(4)Select the acquisition address fill in the input gateway in what data format according to the requirements. OCT/DEC/HEX are octal/decimal/hexadecimal respectively. Mitsubishi FX5U X and Y data points are octal, so choose octal. Select according to the needs, such as register W is hexadecimal, register D is decimal.
(5) Address type: Selected according to the collected PLC data points. Collect the data of "Y7", select "Y".
(6) Address: the register address of the collected data point, Collecting the data of "Y7", the address is: 7.
(7) Data type: According to the type of register selected by PLC.
(8) Add Number: If it is to collect continuous addresses, the same register can be collected multiple times.
(9) Read/Write: choose from "read only", "read and write".
(10) Ratio: Set the ratio to be multiplied or minified for uploading to cloud
(11) Mapping Address: Enter the address where the collected data points will be stored in the gateway device. The address can be set freely, but mapping addresses must be unique. In the configuration software, the mapping address shown outside represents the Modbus address, while the value in parentheses, M.XXX, indicates the PLC Modbus address.
(12) Variable unit: Fill in arbitrarily according to requirements, or not fill in.
(13) COV Precision: Set the change threshold that triggers a COV notification. This field only needs to be filled in when the COV notification function is enabled on uplink BACnet/IP.
(14) Click "OK".
Note: After clicking "OK", the data points will be displayed in the box as shown in the figure above. If you want to continue adding data points, right-click on the box and click "Add" to enter data point configuration box, repeat (2)--(14) Steps.
Note: Clicking "Write Configuration" will restart the gateway automatically, and the data points collected from FX5U will take effect only after restarting.

Collecting OMRON PLC Data
Add OMRON PLC to COM Port
Select the COM port according to the serial port board, because the gateway device 485 interface is 2-wire, if the serial port board is 485, pay attention to the serial port board DIP switch to select 2-wire or 4-wire.
COM Port Configuration
Takes the serial port board CP1W-CIF11 and Omron CP1L-L as examples, the serial port board DIP switches 2 and 3 are ON, the DIP switch SW4 of CP1L-L is OFF, the serial port board and other DIP switches of the PLC The location is set as required. The serial port board RDA- or SDA- is connected to the gateway COM2 B, and RDB+or SDB+the gateway COM2 A. CP1L-L serial port configuration mode should select Host Link.
COM2 Configuration as shown

(2) Mode selection: Collection.
(3) Since the example collection is Omron CP1L-L, the equipment brand: select "OMRON" from the drop-down box, and the equipment model: CJ/CS/CP. The polling interval and communication timeout are set according to requirements.
(4) The baud rate, stop bit, data bit, and parity bit are configured according to the parameters of the Omron CP1L-L serial port, which are consistent with them. Viewed from the Omron programming software, the Omron serial port selects the standard baud rate: 9600, stop bit: 2, data bit: 7, parity bit: Even.
(5) Click "OK".
Note: Click "Write Configuration" and the gateway device will restart automatically, and the configuration of the COM port will take effect after restarting.
Add CP1L to COM Port
(1) Click "COM2", click the right mouse button, click "Add" to enter device configuration box.
(2) Fill in the device name arbitrarily, such as: CP1L-L.
(3) Device address: CP1L-L serial port unit number, fill in as required, the address must be consistent with the unit number set by CP1L-L, otherwise communication will fail.
(4) Click "OK" to add the CP1L-L device.
Note: After clicking OK, the added devices will be displayed under COM2, as shown in the figure above, CP1L-L. If you want to add multiple devices, repeat steps (1)-(4).
Note: Click "Write Configuration" to restart the gateway device automatically. After restarting, the CP1L-L device with COM2 port is added successfully.
Add CP1L Datapoints
Take the CIO register of CP1L as an example
(1) Click "CP1L-L", move the mouse cursor to the box, right-click the mouse, and click "Add" to enter data point setting box.
(2) Variable name: Name the data point, such as: CIO2.01.
(3) The identifier of the data point can be filled in arbitrarily. The identifier cannot be repeated. For example, the identifier of the CIO2.01 data point is filled in as CIO201. Some platform identifiers cannot recognize the decimal point.
(4) Select the acquisition address fill in the input gateway in what data format according to the requirements. OCT/DEC/HEX are octal/decimal/hexadecimal respectively. The CIO register is collected in bits with decimals, so the decimal system is selected.
(5) Address type: Select according to Omron's register, if you want to collect "CIO2.01", select "CIO_BIT".
(6) Address: The register address of the collected data point, collecting "CIO2.01", so fill in "2.01".

(8) Add Number: The number of acquisitions. If it is to acquire continuous addresses, the same register can be acquired multiple times.
(9) Read/Write: According to PLC register selection. Select from "Read Only", "Read and Write".

In the configuration software, the mapping address shown outside represents the Modbus address, while the value in parentheses, M.XXX, represents the PLC Modbus address.
(11) Variable unit: fill in arbitrarily according to requirements, or not fill in.
(12) COV Precision: Set the change threshold for triggering a COV notification. This field only needs to be completed when the COV notification function is enabled on uplink BACnet/IP.
(13) Click "OK.
Note: After clicking "OK", the data points will be displayed in the box as shown in the figure above. If you want to continue adding data points, right-click on the box and click "Add" to enter data point configuration box, repeat (2)--(13) Steps.

In the configuration software, the mapping address shown outside represents the Modbus address, while the value in parentheses, M.XXX, indicates the PLC Modbus address.
Variable unit: Fill in according to requirements, or not fill in.
COV Precision: Set the change threshold that triggers a COV notification. This field only needs to be filled in when the COV notification function is enabled on uplink BACnet/IP.
Click OK to confirm.
Note: After confirming the configuration, datapoints will appear in the box like above picture. To add more datapoints, right click the box and click Add to enter configuration box, repeat Step (2)-(14)
Note: Click Write Configuration. Gateway will restart automatically and CP1L-EL datapoint is added successfully.
Uploading Data to Platforms
Regardless of the protocol used for data collection, the configuration for each platform remains the same. Please refer to section 5.5 "Configuring Data Upload to Various Platforms" for guidance.
Collecting Delta PLC Data
Add Delta PLC to COM Port
The configuration method of the 8 COM ports is the same. Take COM2 connection as an example. DVP-12SA2 COM3+ is connected to gateway COM2 A, COM3- is connected to gateway COM2 B.
COM Port Configuration
The configuration parameters for the DVP-12SA2 COM3 are as follows:
Configure the COM2 port according to the configuration parameters of DVP-12SA2 COM3
(1) Double-click "COM2" to enter COM setting box.
(2) Mode selection: Collection.
(3) Because it is a collection of Delta DVP-12SA2, the device brand: select DELTA from the drop-down box, and the device model: DELTA_DVP. The polling interval and communication timeout in the button are set according to requirements.
(4) The baud rate, stop bit, data bit and parity bit are configured according to the parameters of the COM3 port of DVP-12SA2, which are consistent with them. DVP-12SA2 COM3 port parameters Baud rate: 9600, stop bit: 1, data bit: 7, parity bit: Even.
(5) Click "OK".
Note: Click "Write Configuration" and the gateway device will restart automatically, and the configuration of the COM port will take effect after restarting.
Add DVP-12SA2 to COM Port
(1) Click "COM2", click the right mouse button, click "Add", to enter device setting box.
(2) Fill in the device name arbitrarily, such as: DVP-12SA2.
(3) Device address: The station number of Delta COM, fill in as required, the address must be consistent with the Delta COM setting, otherwise communication will fail.
(4) Click "OK" to add DVP-12SA2 device.
Note: After clicking OK, the added devices will be displayed under COM2, as shown in the figure above. DVP-12SA2, if you want to add multiple devices, repeat steps (1)-(4).
Note: Clicking "Write Configuration" will restart the gateway device automatically. After restarting, the DVP-12SA2 device with COM2 port added will be added successfully.
Add DVP-12SA2 Datapoints
Taking adding data points Y0 and D0 as an example, the address of register X and Y of Delta DVP-12SA2 is octal, and the address of register D is decimal.
(1) Click "DVP-12SA2", move the mouse cursor to the box, right-click the mouse, and click "Add" to enter data point setting box.
(2) Variable name: Name the data point, such as: Y0.
(3) The identifier of the data point can be filled in arbitrarily. The identifier cannot be repeated, for example, the identifier of the Y0 data point is filled in as Y0.
(4) Select the collection address data format filled in the input gateway according to the requirements. OCT/DEC/HEX are octal/decimal/hexadecimal respectively. Register X and Y addresses are in octal, and register D addresses are in decimal.
(5) Address type: According to Delta's register selection, if you want to collect "Y0", select "Y".
(6) Address: The register address of the collected data point, such as: collecting "Y0", so fill in "0".
(7) Data type: Select according to the acquisition PLC register, such as: "Y0" is the coil type, so select "bool".
(8) Add Number: The number of acquisitions. If it is to acquire continuous addresses, the same register can be acquired multiple times.
(9) Read/Write: According to PLC register selection. Select from "Read Only", "Read and Write".
(10) Mapping Address: Enter the address where the collected data points will be stored in the gateway device. The address can be set freely, but mapping addresses must be unique. For example, if collecting Y0, it can be stored at register address 10 in the gateway. In the configuration software, the mapping address shown outside represents the Modbus address, while the value in parentheses, M.XXX, indicates the PLC Modbus address.
(11) Variable unit: Fill in arbitrarily according to requirements, or not fill in.
(12) COV Precision: Set the change threshold to trigger a COV notification. This field only needs to be filled in when the COV notification function is enabled on the uplink BACnet/IP.
(13) Click "OK".
Note: After clicking "OK", the data points will be displayed in the box as shown in the figure above. If you want to continue adding data points, right-click on the box and click "Add" to enter data point configuration box, repeat (2)--(13) Steps.
Note: Clicking "Write Configuration" will restart the gateway automatically, and the data points added by DVP-12SA2 will take effect only after restarting.
Collecting BACnet Data
The gateway supports nine types of object collection: AI, AO, AV, BI, BO, BV, MSI, MSO, and MSV. The collected values represent the current value attributes of these nine types of objects.
Add BACnet MS/TP Device to COM Port
COM Port Configuration
Taking COM2 as an example for collecting data from a BACnet MS/TP device. The COM2 485 interface's A+ and B- correspond respectively to the BACnet MS/TP device's 485 interface's A+ and B-. For communication stability, BACnet MS/TP devices require termination resistors, and the gateway’s polling interval should be set to over 800ms.
COM2 settings as below,
(1) Double-click on "COM2"
(2) Select the mode: Collection.
(3) Since it is for collecting data from a BACnet MS/TP device, select "BACnet" from the device brand dropdown menu and choose "BACnet MS/TP" as the device model. Set the polling interval and communication timeout according to your requirements, the polling interval needs to be set to more than 800ms.
(4) Local MAC: Enter the gateway's BACnet MS/TP MAC address. Be sure to avoid conflicts with other BACnet MS/TP devices.
(5)Configure the baud rate, stop bits, data bits, and parity bits based on the parameters of the BACnet MS/TP device's serial port, ensuring consistency.
(6) Click "OK".
Note: Clicking "Write Configuration" will automatically restart the gateway device. After the restart, the configuration of the COM port will take effect.
Add BACnet MS/TP Device
(1) Click on "COM2", then right-click the mouse and select "Add".
(2) Fill in any device name, such as "BACnet MS/TP".
(3) Device Address: Enter the ID of the BACnet MS/TP device.
(4) Click "OK" to add the BACnet MS/TP device.
Note: After clicking "OK," the added device will be displayed under COM2 as shown in the above image, labeled as BACnet MS/TP. If you want to add multiple devices,  repeat steps (1) to (4).
Note: Clicking "Write Configuration" will automatically restart the gateway device. After the restart, the added BACnet MS/TP device on COM2 will be successfully added.
Add BACnet MS/TP Device Datapoints
Configuring analoginput object collection as follows:
Click on "BACnet MS/TP", move the mouse cursor to the box, right-click, then select "Add".
(2) Variable Name: Name the data point, for example, if collecting data from "analog input 1", you can name it "analoginput".
(3) The MQTT Identifier for the data point can be filled in arbitrarily. The identifier must be unique. For example, if collecting data from "analog input 1", you can fill in the identifier as "analoginput".
(4) Choose the collection address format based on your requirements and input it into the gateway. OCT/DEC/HEX represent octal/decimal/hexadecimal formats respectively. For BACnet MS/TP data points, select decimal format.
(5) Address Type: Select based on the object type being collected from BACnet MS/TP. For example, if collecting data from "analog input 1", choose "analog input".
(6) Address: Object instance number. For example, if collecting data from "analog input 1", the object instance number is: 1.
(7) Data Type: Choose based on the property of the current value attribute of the selected BACnet MS/TP device object being collected.
(8) Add Number: If collecting consecutive addresses, multiple collections can be made from the same register.
(9) Read/Write: Choose from "Read-only" or "Read/Write".
(10) Ratiot: Indicates how much the value should be multiplied or divided before being uploaded to the platform. Fill in as per requirements.
(11) Mapping Address: Enter the address where the collected data points will be stored in the gateway device. The address can be set freely, but mapping addresses must be unique. For example, if collecting “analoginput 1”, its mapping address can be set to “0”. In the configuration software, the mapping address shown outside represents the Modbus address, while the value in parentheses, M.XXX, indicates the PLC Modbus address.
(12) Variable Unit: Fill in as needed, it can be left blank.
(13) Click "OK".
Note: After clicking "OK", the data point will be displayed in the box. If you wish to continue adding data points, right-click inside the box, select "Add" to open the data point configuration box, and repeat steps (2) to (13).
Note: Clicking "Write Configuration" will automatically restart the gateway. After the restart, the collection of data points from BACnet MS/TP devices will take effect.
Add BACnet/IP Device to Ethernet Port
Both the WAN port and LAN port can collect data from BACnet/IP devices. They can directly connect to BACnet/IP devices or collect data through a switch. However, the WAN port and LAN port cannot collect data from BACnet/IP devices simultaneously.
WAN Port Configuration
This example illustrates collecting data from BACnet/IP devices through a switch connected to the WAN port, with the switch connected to the external network. The configuration for the WAN port is as follows:
(1) Double-click on "WAN".
(2) Auto IP: Indicates whether the WAN port is set to automatically obtain an IP address. It's enabled by default. Adjust as needed. In this example, the switch is connected to a router which assigns IP addresses automatically, so keep this option enabled.
(3) IP Address: The gateway obtains its IP address from the router. If a specific IP address is required, set it accordingly. Ensure that the PLC and the gateway are on the same LAN if a specific IP address is used.
(4) Subnet Mask: The subnet mask for the gateway's WAN port. Set according to requirements if using a specific IP address.
(5) Gateway: The gateway address obtained from the router. Set according to requirements if using a specific IP address.
(6) MAC Address: The MAC address of the gateway.
(7) DNS: The DNS address obtained by the gateway from the router. Set according to requirements if using a specific IP address.
(8) Click "BACnet Enabled" and specify the port number, such as port number "47808", Enter the BACnet/IP ID of the gateway. The ID must not conflict with the BACnet/IP slave device IDs in the local area network.
(9) Click "OK".
Note: Clicking "Write Configuration" will automatically restart the gateway. After the restart, the configuration changes for the WAN port will take effect.
Note: The IP address of the WAN port determines which network segment the WAN port device belongs to. If the IP address of the WAN port device does not belong to the network segment set for the WAN port, data collection through the WAN port will not work. You may need to adjust either the IP address of the WAN port or the IP address of the WAN port device based on your requirements. Remember to write configuration and restart the device for any IP address changes to take effect.
Add BACnet/IP Device to WAN Port
(1) Click on "WAN", then right-click the mouse and select "Add".
(2) Device Name: Name the device, for example, since we are collecting data from BACnet/IP devices, you can name it "BACnet".
(3) Leave the device IP and device port blank.
(4) Since we are collecting data from BACnet/IP devices, select "BACnet" as the device brand and choose "BACnet/IP" as the device model. The polling interval and communication timeout can be left as default or adjusted as needed.
(5) Device Address: According to your requirements.
(6) Click "OK" to add the BACnet device.
Note: After clicking "OK", the added device will be displayed under the WAN port. If you wish to add multiple devices, repeat steps (1) to (6).
Note: Clicking "Write Configuration" will automatically restart the gateway. After the restart, the BACnet devices added under the WAN port will be successfully added.
Add BACnet/IP Device Datapoints
Configuring for collecting binaryinput objects as follows:
(1) Click on "BACnet", then right-click inside the box and select "Add".
(2) Variable Name: Name the data point, for example, if collecting data from "binary input 0", you can name it "binaryinput".
(3) Variable Key: Fill in arbitrarily. The identifier must be unique. For example, if collecting data from "binary input 0", the identifier can be "binaryinput".
(4) Choose the collection address format based on your requirements and input it into the gateway. OCT/DEC/HEX represent octal/decimal/hexadecimal formats respectively. For BACnet/IP data points, select decimal format.
(5) Address Type: Select based on the object type being collected from BACnet/IP. For example, if collecting data from "binaryinput 0", choose "binaryinput".
(6) Address: Object instance number. For example, if collecting data from "binary input 0", the object instance number is: 0.
(7) Data Type: Choose based on the property of the current value attribute of the selected BACnet/IP device object being collected.
(8) Add Number: If collecting consecutive addresses, multiple collections can be made from the same register.
(9) Read/Write Type: Choose from "Read-only" or "Read/Write".
(10) Mapping Address: Enter the address where the collected data points will be stored in the gateway device. The address can be set freely, but mapping addresses must be unique. For example, if collecting “binaryinput 0”, its mapping address can be set to “1”. In the configuration software, the mapping address shown outside represents the Modbus address, while the value in parentheses, M.XXX, indicates the PLC Modbus address.
(11) Variable Unit: Fill in as needed or leave blank.
(12) Click "OK".
Note: After clicking "OK", the data point will be displayed in the box. If you wish to continue adding data points, right-click inside the box, select "Add" to open the data point configuration box, and repeat steps (2) to (12).
Note: Clicking "Write Configuration" will automatically restart the gateway. After the restart, the collection of BACnet data points will take effect.

COM1 to COM8 can all be used as slaves. Taking COM1 as an example for Modbus RTU Slave, ensure that there are no functional conflicts with the COM ports.
Operation Steps:

5.5.5 BACnet/IP Configuration
For example, collecting Modbus data and converting it to BACnet/IP.
Operation Steps:
(1) Double-click "BACnet/IP" to open the BACnet/IP configuration dialog.
(2) Click the "Enable" button to activate BACnet/IP. Default: Disabled. Gray means disabled, green means enabled.
(3) Network Interface: Choose the "WAN" port. Click "WAN" to check that the WAN port's IP address is: 192.168.1.155.
(4) Port: Set the BACnet/IP UDP port, default is 47808.

(6) Vendor Identification: You can enter any ID.

(9) Object Description: A description of the gateway, can be entered as desired.

The object instance corresponds to the Modbus address listed in the mapping address field on the data points page of the configuration software.
For example, using the data point with function code 04 at address "0", if the mapping address in the configuration software is "3", then the data point's address in BACnet/IP will be: Analog_Input:3.

(2) Click the "Enable" button to activate BACnet/MSTP. Default: Disabled. Gray means disabled, green means enabled.
(3) COM: Choose according to your needs, default is COM1. Make sure the selected COM port for BACnet MS/TP does not conflict with other functions.

Modbus protocol data is provided via the current values of AI, AO, BI, and BO object properties. For other protocols, data is provided via the current values of AO and BO object properties. 5.5.11 MQTT Client
The "BLIIOT" JSON data format of MQTT Client and MQTT Client II is the same as BLIIOT MQTT. Refer to：BLIIOT MQTT Data Format
Connect to the ThingsBoard platform, select the JSON data format in the "thingsboard-telemetry-gateway" format. The ThingsBoard platform domain name is thingsboard.cloud.
Connect to a platform that supports Sparkplug B, such as the ignition platform, select the JSON data format in the "sparkplug b" format, click the button next to the data template item, enter configuration box to configure the group ID and edge node ID.
The difference between MQTT Client and MQTT Client II is that the subscription topic of MQTT Client II does not work. The purpose of MQTT Client II is that the platform can view the data but cannot control the data. Therefore, MQTT Client II connection is not introduced.
The configuration of MQTT Client is as follows: Connection without certificate and the JSON data format in BLIIOT format as an example.
(1) Double-click "MQTT Client" to enter configuration box. (2) Click the Enable button to enable MQTT Client. Default: off. Gray: Disabled, Green: enabled. (3) IP/domain: fill in the IP/domain name of the MQTT server. 4) Port: Fill in the MQTT server port, Default: 1883. (5) Client ID: The client identifier used in the MQTT connection message, and the server uses the client identifier to identify the client. (6) Username: The username used in the MQTT connection message, the server can use it for authentication and authorization. (7) Password: The password used in the MQTT connection message, which can be used by the server for authentication and authorization. (8) Data template: Select according to the JSON data format supported by the MQTT server, default is "Beilai". (9) Subscribe topic: The topic name used by the MQTT subscription message. After subscription, the server can send a publish message to the client for control. (10) Publish topic: The topic name used by MQTT to publish the message. The topic name is used to identify which information channel the payload data should be published to. (11) Upload cycle: Data publish interval, default is 30S. (12) Data retransmission: Whether to enable data retransmission, Gray: disabled, Green: enabled. (13) Data Reporting Settings: Select the data point to be uploaded in the box on the right side of the configuration box, the default is blank means all upload.
(14) Click "OK" to confirm the configuration of BLIIOT MQTT. (15) Click "Write Configuration", the MQTT Client will not be enabled until the gateway device restarts. Re-open the configuration software to log in to the device, and you can see on the basic information page that the prompt light of "MQTT Client Online Status" is green, indicating that the MQTT Client is connected. The rightmost shows the online status of the slave device.

(8) Client certificate file: Select the client certificate file to upload. (9) Client key file: Select the client key file to upload. (10) Publish topic: the topic created when creating the rule, the topic name used by MQTT to publish the message, click "Add" to fill in the publishing topic name. You can fill in multiple publishing topics, select a publishing topic, and click "Delete" to delete the selected topic. For example: the topic viewed in the "rule" of "action" is "iot/topic", so fill in"iot/topic".
(11) Upload cycle: Data publish interval, the default is 30S. (12) Data Reporting Setting: Select the data point to be uploaded in the box on the right side of the configuration box, the default is blank means all upload. (13) Click "OK" to confirm the configuration of Amazon Cloud. (14) Click "Write Configuration", and Amazon Cloud will be enabled after the gateway device restarts. Re-open the configuration software to log in to the device, and on the basic information page, you can see that the “Amazon Cloud Online Status” indicator light is green, indicating that the Amazon cloud is connected. The rightmost shows the online status of the slave device.
5.5.18 View and Send Command in AWS
Login to AWS, click Act, click Test and select subscription topic “iot/topic” to view messages published by gateway
5.5.19 BLIIOT Cloud via Modbus
Double click BLIIOT Modbus IoT to enter configuration window
Click Enable to enable(green) BLIIOT cloud via Modbus. Default is disabled (Gray)
Choose Modbus RTU or Modbus TCP
Server IP/Domain Name: modbus.dtuip.com. (Automatic filling in default)
Server Port: 6651 (Automatic filling in default)
Modbus Station: Set Gateway Modbus communication address
Login Message: Input device serial number issued by BLIIOT.
Login ACK Message: Not necessary for BLIIOT cloud connection
Heartbeat Message: Q (Automatic filling in default)
Heartbeat ACK Message: A(Automatic filling in default)
Heartbeat Interval: Set cycle time of sending Heartbeat message. Default is 60s
Click OK to confirm the configuration.
Click Write Configuration. Gateway will restart and BLIIOT Cloud via Modbus is enabled successfully. Open configuration software and login device. BLIIOT cloud via Modbus connection status can be viewed from basic information. Green indicates device is connected BLIIOT cloud via Modbus. Slave devices connection status can be viewed from the right box.
5.5.20 View Data in BLIIOT Cloud via Modbus
Configure datapoint in cloud like below picture. First create datapoint, then enter connection setting and put datapoint Modbus ID, function code, address, data format, byte sequence and collecting cycle. Modbus address in BLIIOT cloud and configuration software is deviated by 1. For example, IEC104 single point remote signal data point 10 has a Modbus mapped address of "0" in the configuration software. Therefore, in BLIIOT Cloud, the offset for this item should be filled with "1". The names of sensors uploaded to the platform can be different from those configured in the software.

5.5.23 BLIIOT MQTT Data Format
The "BLIIOT" JSON data format of MQTT Client and MQTT Client II is the same as that of BLIIOT MQTT. The details are as follows
Valid Load Data Format in device Publishing messages
Note: // Read/write identifier: The character is "flag", followed by the "MQTT identifier of the data point", which is the MQTT identifier filled in when adding the data point, and can be defined independently.
//Data Type and Value:
Boolean data: Character is "switcher", followed by "0" or "1"(0 represents open, 1 represents close)
Numeric Data: character is "value", followed by actual value
GPS positioning data: GPS latitude character is "lat", followed by "specific value"
GPS longitude character is "lng" followed by "specific value"
//Alarm, Recover mark, character is "state", followed by "alarm" or "recovery"(alarm represents alarm data, recovery represents alarm recovery data)
//Gateway name identification: the character is "gateway_indentify", followed by "gateway name".
//Time mark: character is "time", followed by actually data uploading timestamp
//Time mark, character is "addtime", followed by "gateway time"
//Retransmission mark: character is "retransmit", followed by "enable"
Offline collected data will be temporarily saved in gateway device. Once network resmues, the data will be retransmitted. Use “retransmit” mark for historical data (MQTT Data Retransmission must be enabled in configuration software)
Valid Load Data Format in device Subscribing messages
Note:
//cloud sensor ID: character is "sensorsID", followed by ID (automatically generated by cloud. Not necessary if it’s self-built cloud)
//Data Type and Value:
Boolean Data: character is "switcher", followed by "0" or "1"
(0 represents open, 1 represents close)
Numeric Data: character is "value", followed by "actual value"
//Read-Write Mark: character is "flag", followed by "datapoint MQTT flag"
//Cloud Downlink Message Mark: character is "down", followed by "down", representing cloud downlink data.
Note: Boolean data will not have double quotation mark, numeric data will have double quotation mark.
Firmware Upgrade
BLIIOT gateways support firmware upgrades through configuration software. Click "About" in the configuration software, then select "Firmware Upgrade" and choose the "update" folder. Click "OK" to start the upgrade. Once the upgrade is complete, a notification box will appear. Click "OK" in the notification box to finish. The "update" folder must be obtained by contacting customer support.

Version History
Overview
The BL118 Series is an industrial-grade, high-performance, scalable, and customizable industrial IoT gateway that comes pre-installed with the Node-RED visual programming environment. It is equipped with a dual-core Cortex-A7 processor and offers flexible configurations for RAM, ROM, and I/O interfaces, supporting WiFi, 4G network communications. It is widely used in smart factories, smart energy, smart cities, smart transportation, and other industrial IoTsolutions, as well as in industrial visualization and digital transformation scenarios.
BL118 Series adopts an industrial-grade design with an onboard independent hardware watchdog and electrical isolation technology. It has passed EMC/EMI tests and has been validated for high and low-temperature performance in the range of -45°C to 80°C, ensuring stable long-term operation in harsh environments.
BL118 Series provides 4/8 isolated RS485/232/CAN serial ports, as well as expandable GPIO, DI, DO, AI, AO, RTD, TC, and other interfaces.
BL118 Series comes pre-installed with the Node-RED visual programming environment, supporting various PLC protocols from major brands, Modbus RTU/ASCII, TCP/IP, BACnet, CAN, OPC UA, MQTT, HTTP, WebSocket, and other industrial protocols. With an easy drag-and-drop node programming approach and an open operating system environment, it is convenientfor customers to carry out secondary development and functional expansion, providing reliable solutions for Industrial IoT (IIoT).
Key Features:
Powerful Performance
Dual-core Cortex-A7 processor, with a clock speed of up to 1.2GHz
Independent hardware watchdog + EMC/EMI anti-interference design, ensuring stable long-term operation
Optional 512M/1G memory and 4G/8G Flash storage
Supports expandable SD card for enhanced historical data storage
Pre-installed Node-RED environment, supports Ubuntu 20.04 system
Supports Gigabit + 100M Ethernet port, expandable WiFi/4G wireless network
Provides 4/8 isolated RS485/232/CAN, 2×USB2.0
Supports GPIO/DI/DO/AI/AO/RTD/TC expansion I/O interfaces
No-Code Development Platform
Pre-installed BLIIOT customized Node-RED, drag-and-drop programming
Built-in industrial protocol node libraries for Modbus/PLC/MQTT
Provides BLRAT remote maintenance tool, supports unmanned operation
Simple development, visual workflow orchestration, no coding experience required
Real-time debugging interface for quick logic verification
Over 5000 official + third-party nodes
Supports Docker container deployment
Built-in support for MQTT/OPC UA/Modbus/PLC protocols
Expandable Web dashboard (charts/widgets/real-time data) for industrial visualization
Typical Application Areas
Smart Factory: PLC Data Acquisition + MES/SCADA System Integration and Industrial Visualization
Energy Management: Aggregation of electricity meters, water meters, and sensor data to thecloud
Smart City: Distributed Device Monitoring and Remote Maintenance

Product Selection
The Node-Red IIoT Gateways adopt a flexible design concept, allowing users to customizeROM and RAM combinations by choosing different System-on-Module(SOM) boards as needed. Additionally, various X and Y boards can be selected to achieve diverse IO configurations,catering to the requirements of different application scenarios.
Product naming convention
Host Model Number - SOM Model Number - X Board Model Number - Y1 Board ModelNumber - Y2 Board Model Number
For example: BL118-SOM334-X1
BL118 indicates 2 Ethernet ports, 2 USB 2.0 ports, an X-board with 2x5PIN, and no Y-board; SOM334 indicates 8GB eMMC, 512MB DDR4, and a wide temperature range of -20~70℃; X1 indicates that the X-board features 4 RS485 ports.
If you need to add WiFi, then you would append "W" to the host model number.
For example: BL118W-SOM334-X1
If you need to add a 4G module, you would append "L" to the host model number.
For example: BL118L-SOM334-X1
BL118 Model List
BL118 SOM Model List
You can select the appropriate ROM, RAM, and temperature grade based on your requirements.
X Series IO Board Model List
You can select the appropriate X series IO board based on your requirements, ensuring that the number of pins on the X series IO board is compatible with the enclosure.
Y Series IO Board Model List
You can select the appropriate Y series IO board based on your requirements, as the Y series IO modulesare compatible with all Y slots. When the Y63 is selected, you can not choose second Y-series IO board.
Wireless Module Selection Table 1: WiFi Module Selection Table
You can choose the WiFi module according to your needs. You can select either the WiFi module or the 4G module, and the selection includes an antenna.
Wireless Module Selection Table 2: 4G Module Selection Table
You can choose the WiFi module according to your needs. You can select either the WiFi module or the 4G module, and the selection includes an antenna.

Introduction
Overview
BL118 Node-RED gateway is an industrial-grade, high-performance, scalable, and customizable edge computing gateway. It comes pre-installed with the Node-RED visual programming environment and features a built-in dual-core A7 processor. The gateway offers flexible configuration of RAM, ROM, and I/O interfaces, and supports WiFi, 4G network communication. It is widely used in industrial IoT solutions and scenarios such as smart factories, smart energy, smart cities, and smart transportation, supporting industrial visualization and digital transformation.
BL118 series adopts an industrial-grade design, featuring a built-in independent hardware watchdog and electrical isolation technology. It has passed EMC and EMI testing, as well as high and low temperature validation ranging from -45°C to 80°C, ensuring long-term stable operation in harsh environments.
BL118 series offers 4 or 8 isolated RS485/232/CAN serial ports, along with expandable interfaces including GPIO, DI, DO, AI, AO, RTD, and TC.
BL118 series comes pre-installed with the Node-RED and supports a wide range of industrial protocols, including major PLC protocols, Modbus RTU/ASCII, TCP/IP, BACnet, CAN, OPC UA, MQTT, HTTP, and WebSocket. With a simple drag-and-drop node-based programming interface and an open operating system environment, it facilitates secondary development and function expansion, providing a reliable solution for the Industrial Internet of Things (IIoT).
Package Contents
Before installing and using the BL118, please make sure the product package includes the following items:
1×BL118 Node-RED Gateway
1×DIN35 Mounting Bracket Set
Pre-installed BL118 Visual Management Software
Pre-installed Node-RED
Pre-installed BLRAT Software
Ubuntu File System
Tool-free Terminal Blocks
If Wi-Fi or 4G modules are selected, the corresponding antennas will be included.
Note: If any of the above items are missing or damaged, please contact us.
Features
Downlink Support: Various PLC protocols, Modbus RTU Master, Modbus TCP Master, DL/T645, IEC101, IEC104, BACnet IP, BACnet MS/TP, and more.
Uplink Support: Modbus TCP, MQTT, OPC UA, BACnet IP, Huawei Cloud IoT, Alibaba Cloud IoT, AWS IoT, ThingsBoard, Sparkplug B, Beilai Cloud, and more.
Wide Operating Voltage Design: Supports DC 12–36V power input with terminal block connection and built-in reverse polarity protection.
Dual-core Cortex-A7 processor with a clock speed of up to 1.2GHz.
Independent Hardware Watchdog + EMC/EMI Interference Protection Design.
Supports optional 512MB or 1GB RAM, and 4GB or 8GB Flash storage.
Allows SD card expansion to enhance historical data storage capacity.
Comes with a pre-installed Node-RED environment and supports the Ubuntu 20.04 operating system.
Equipped with Gigabit and Fast Ethernet ports, and supports expandable WiFi and 4G wireless networks.
Provides 4 or 8 isolated RS485/232/CAN serial ports and two USB 2.0 ports.
Supports GPIO/DI/DO/AI/AO/RTD/TC Expandable I/O.
Hardware Specifications
Note: If ETF level 3 is required, you need to purchase our filter module separately.
Software Specifications
Model Selection
BL118 Node-RED Edge gateway, designed for secondary development, adopts a flexible architecture that allows users to select different SOM boards to configure various ROM and RAM combinations. By integrating different X and Y boards, it supports a wide range of I/O combinations to meet the needs of diverse application scenarios.
Product Naming Convention:
Host Model–SOM Model–X Board Model–Y1 Board Model–Y2 Board Model
For example: BL118-SOM334-X1
BL118 indicates host model with 2 Ethernet ports, 2 USB 2.0 ports, an X board using a 2x5 PIN interface, and no Y boards.
SOM334 specifies an eMMC of 4GB, 512MB DDR4 RAM, and a wide temperature range of -40°C to 85°C.
X1 indicates that the X board provides 4 channels of RS485.
To indicate additional modules:
Add "W" after the host model for Wi-Fi support (e.g., BL118W-SOM334-X1).
Add "L" after the host model for 4G module support (e.g., BL118L-SOM334-X1).
